aiTitle: Plan week 7 task from docs and GitHub epic

content.text: Plan the week 7 task from @docs/progress.md , HARNESS_TODO.md and github epic and milestones

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- code-simplifier:code-simplifier: Simplifies and refines code for clarity, consistency, and maintainability while preserving all functionality. Focuses on recently modified code unless instructed otherwise. (Tools: All tools)

- docs-consistency-guard: Documentation agent that audits docs for staleness and inconsistency AND generates/updates documentation on request. Use proactively when reviewing any PR that touches CLAUDE.md, docs/adr/, CONTRIBUTING.md, or docs/architecture.md. Also invoke when the user asks "is the documentation up to date?", "update the milestone", "scaffold the ADR for X", "add this to the ADR index", "add a TODO item", "scaffold a README for X", or "create a doc stub for Y." (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash, Write, Edit)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches ظ¤ when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- migration-safety: Specialized reviewer for Alembic migrations under backend/alembic/versions/. Audits for backward-incompatible operations that would break running code during a deploy, per working-agreement (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash)

- orchestration-abstraction-guard: Specialized reviewer that audits backend code for provider-specific branching that bypasses the OrchestrationProvider abstraction. Use this proactively when reviewing any PR that touches backend/app/orchestration/, backend/app/services/, or backend/app/api/ ظ¤ especially any code path handling ADF or Airflow events, trigger bindings, or pipeline_runs. Also invoke when the user asks "does this go through the abstraction?" or similar. (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- pr-review-toolkit:code-reviewer: Use this agent when you need to review code for adherence to project guidelines, style guides, and best practices. This agent should be used proactively after writing or modifying code, especially before committing changes or creating pull requests. It will check for style violations, potential issues, and ensure code follows the established patterns in CLAUDE.md. Also the agent needs to know which files to focus on for the review. In most cases this will be recently completed work which is unstaged in git (can be retrieved by running git diff). However there can be cases where this is different, make sure to specify this as the agent input when calling the agent. Typical triggers include the user asking for a review of a feature they just implemented, the assistant proactively reviewing its own newly-written code before declaring a task done, and a final pre-PR check before opening a pull request. See "When to invoke" in the agent body for worked scenarios. (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:code-simplifier: Use this agent when code has been written or modified and needs to be simplified for clarity, consistency, and maintainability while preserving all functionality. This agent should be triggered automatically after completing a coding task or writing a logical chunk of code. It simplifies code by following project best practices while retaining all functionality. The agent focuses only on recently modified code unless instructed otherwise.

Examples:

<example>
Context: The assistant has just implemented a new feature that adds user authentication to an API endpoint.
user: "Please add authentication to the /api/users endpoint"
assistant: "I've implemented the authentication for the /api/users endpoint. Here's the code:"
<function call omitted for brevity>
<commentary>
Since a logical chunk of code has been written (authentication feature), use the Task tool to launch the code-simplifier agent to improve the code's clarity and maintainability while preserving functionality.
</commentary>
assistant: "Now let me use the code-simplifier agent to refine this implementation for better clarity and maintainability"
</example>

<example>
Context: The assistant has just fixed a bug by adding several conditional checks.
user: "Fix the null pointer exception in the data processor"
assistant: "I've added the necessary null checks to prevent the exception:"
<function call omitted for brevity>
<commentary>
After modifying code to fix a bug, use the code-simplifier agent to ensure the fix follows best practices and maintains code quality.
</commentary>
assistant: "Let me refine this bug fix using the code-simplifier agent to ensure it follows our best practices"
</example>

<example>
Context: The assistant has just refactored a function to improve performance.
user: "Optimize the data sorting algorithm for better performance"
assistant: "I've optimized the sorting algorithm. Here's the updated implementation:"
<function call omitted for brevity>
<commentary>
After completing a performance optimization task, use the code-simplifier agent to ensure the optimized code is also clear and maintainable.
</commentary>
assistant: "Now I'll use the code-simplifier agent to ensure the optimized code is also clear and follows our coding standards"
</example> (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:comment-analyzer: Use this agent when you need to analyze code comments for accuracy, completeness, and long-term maintainability. This includes (1) after generating large documentation comments or docstrings, (2) before finalizing a pull request that adds or modifies comments, (3) when reviewing existing comments for potential technical debt or comment rot, and (4) when you need to verify that comments accurately reflect the code they describe. See "When to invoke" in the agent body for worked scenarios. (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:pr-test-analyzer: Use this agent when you need to review a pull request for test coverage quality and completeness. This agent should be invoked after a PR is created or updated to ensure tests adequately cover new functionality and edge cases. Typical triggers include the user asking whether tests on a freshly-created PR are thorough, an updated PR adding new logic that needs coverage analysis, and a final pre-merge double-check before marking a PR ready. See "When to invoke" in the agent body for worked scenarios. (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:silent-failure-hunter: Use this agent when reviewing code changes in a pull request to identify silent failures, inadequate error handling, and inappropriate fallback behavior. This agent should be invoked proactively after completing a logical chunk of work that involves error handling, catch blocks, fallback logic, or any code that could potentially suppress errors. Examples:

<example>
Context: Daisy has just finished implementing a new feature that fetches data from an API with fallback behavior.
Daisy: "I've added error handling to the API client. Can you review it?"
Assistant: "Let me use the silent-failure-hunter agent to thoroughly examine the error handling in your changes."
<Task tool invocation to launch silent-failure-hunter agent>
</example>

<example>
Context: Daisy has created a PR with changes that include try-catch blocks.
Daisy: "Please review PR #1234"
Assistant: "I'll use the silent-failure-hunter agent to check for any silent failures or inadequate error handling in this PR."
<Task tool invocation to launch silent-failure-hunter agent>
</example>

<example>
Context: Daisy has just refactored error handling code.
Daisy: "I've updated the error handling in the authentication module"
Assistant: "Let me proactively use the silent-failure-hunter agent to ensure the error handling changes don't introduce silent failures."
<Task tool invocation to launch silent-failure-hunter agent>
</example> (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:type-design-analyzer: Use this agent when you need expert analysis of type design in your codebase. Specifically use it (1) when introducing a new type to ensure it follows best practices for encapsulation and invariant expression, (2) during pull request creation to review all types being added, and (3) when refactoring existing types to improve their design quality. The agent will provide both qualitative feedback and quantitative ratings on encapsulation, invariant expression, usefulness, and enforcement. See "When to invoke" in the agent body for worked scenarios. (Tools: All tools)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

## claude.ai Context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

## claude.ai Hugging Face
You have tools for using the Hugging Face Hub. arXiv paper id's are often used as references between datasets, models and papers. There are over 100 tags in use, common tags include 'Text Generation', 'Transformers', 'Image Classification' and so on.
Hugging Face tools are being used by authenticated user 'TheurgicDuke771'

## claude.ai Microsoft Learn
# Microsoft Learn MCP Server

This server gives structured access to official Microsoft and Azure documentation via three tools:

## Tools

### microsoft_docs_search
Search official documentation and return up to 10 concise, high-quality content chunks (max 500 tokens each), including title, URL, and excerpt.

- Use first to get a quick, reliable overview
- Ideal for grounding answers in Microsoft knowledge

### microsoft_code_sample_search
Search for code snippets and examples in official Microsoft Learn documentation and return up to 20 relevant, high-quality code samples.

- Use when you need to provide sample Microsoft/Azure related code in your answers.  
- Ideal for generating code snippets or practical implementation examples.  
- Optional parameter `language` can filter results. 

### microsoft_docs_fetch
Fetch and convert full Microsoft documentation pages to markdown.

- Use after search when you need full content from a specific URL
- Required for detailed tutorials, troubleshooting, prerequisites, code samples, or when search results are incomplete or outdated

## Workflow

1. Use `microsoft_docs_search` to find relevant documents.
2. If you need code examples or practical snippets, use `microsoft_code_sample_search`.
3. If deeper or complete information is needed, use `microsoft_docs_fetch`.

**Search gives breadth. Code Sample Search gives practical examples. Fetch gives depth.**

All content comes from Microsoft Learn or official sources, returned in clean markdown format.

## context7
Use this server to retrieve up-to-date documentation and code examples for any library.

attachment.content:
- commit-commands:clean_gone: Cleans up all git branches marked as [gone] (branches that have been deleted on the remote but still exist locally), including removing associated worktrees.
- commit-commands:commit-push-pr: Commit, push, and open a PR
- commit-commands:commit: Create a git commit
- code-review:code-review: Code review a pull request
- pr-review-toolkit:review-pr: Comprehensive PR review using specialized agents
- claude-code-setup:claude-automation-recommender: Analyze a codebase and recommend Claude Code automations (hooks, subagents, skills, plugins, MCP servers). Use when user asks for automation recommendations, wants to optimize their Claude Code setup, mentions improving Claude Code workflows, asks how to first set up Claude Code for a project, or wants to know what Claude Code features they should use.
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Guidance for distinctive, intentional visual design when building new UI or reshaping an existing one. Helps with aesthetic direction, typography, and making choices that don't read as templated defaults.
- skill-creator:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; highظْmax: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only ظ¤ it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK ظ¤ model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER ظ¤ read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" ظ¤ whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) ظ¤ never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named ظ¤ don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Reads-only, persists nothing: one aggregate query (row count + null/distinct/min/max per column) + one top-N-values query per column, then `assemble_profile`. **SQL-injection-safe** ظ¤ queries are built with the **SQLAlchemy Core expression language** (`select`/`table`/`column`, dialect-quoted) so there's no raw-string SQL sink (no S608/B608/CodeQL `py/sql-injection`); identifiers are additionally allowlist-validated (`validate_identifier`, strict `^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_$]*$`) as defence-in-depth + a clean early 422, and `top_n` is `int()`-coerced. v1 ظْ 422: non-Snowflake type (dispatch generalises Week 5), bad identifier, no schema; execution failure ظْ 502 (adapter exception never echoed). min/max/top-values NaN-sanitised. 28 tests (22 pure: identifier allowlist incl. injection strings, compiled-SQL builders, assembly, div-by-zero, NaN; 6 endpoint via fake conn: stats, injection-422, unsupported-type-422, 502, edit-gated, no-schema-422); `suites.py` 100%, `profile_service` 90% (live `_open_connection` is the deferred warehouse seam)

Same `POST /suites/{id}/profile` endpoint, now polymorphic: SQL targets pass `table`/`schema`, flat-file targets pass `path` (+ optional `file_format`, else inferred from extension); response carries whichever identity applies. CSV + Parquet. **pandas "load less data" levers** (per the user's scale.html prompt): column projection (CSV `usecols`, Parquet `columns=` via pyarrow schema) so profiling 3 of N columns doesn't read all N, + row sampling (`_SAMPLE_ROWS`), + **zero-copy Arrow-backed dtypes** on the Parquet read (`dtype_backend="pyarrow"` ظ¤ parquet is already Arrow, kept zero-copy; the stat helpers + `_to_native` are Arrow-scalar/`pd.NA`-safe). Stats are vectorised pandas (no Python row loops ظ¤ enhancingperf N/A; CoW is a no-op for a read-only profiler + a process-global flag, so not toggled; pyarrow CSV engine rejected ظ¤ it's incompatible with `nrows`/callable-`usecols`). NaN/TimestampظْJSON-safe. v1 ظْ 422: unsupported type (UC still pending), missing target, unknown format, missing column; read failure ظْ 502 (exception never echoed). Reuses `ColumnProfile`/`ProfileResult`. 24 new tests (pure `profile_dataframe`/`infer_file_format`/`_to_native`/projection-parse + endpoint via fake `_read_dataframe`); `suites.py` 100%, `profile_service` 85% (only the live `_open_connection`/`_download_bytes` network seams uncovered ظ¤ deferred smoke). _Deferred: streaming/range reads (whole object still downloaded before sampling) + out-of-core (Dask) ظ¤ future work if profiling cost bites._

UC's **3-level namespace** (`catalog.schema.table`) is handled by building the table with a `quoted_name(f"{catalog}.{schema}", quote=False)` so the dialect emits three dotted parts rather than quoting the dotted string as one ظ¤ verified by compiling the real builders against the Databricks dialect. Injection-safe: every part (catalog/schema/table/column) is allowlist-validated (no raw-string SQL, no CodeQL py/sql-injection surface). 153	- [x] ظ£à Check types for flat files: schema validation, row count, null checks, ~~freshness by filename date~~ ظ¤ `FlatFileCheckRunner` (`datasources/flatfile.py`): downloads the file (S3/ADLS) into a pandas DataFrame and runs the suite's GX expectations against GX's **pandas DataFrame asset**, so all GX expectation-kind checks (schema/row-count/null/value checks) run against flat files. Extracted the shared GX machinery ظ¤ snake_caseظْGX-class translation, GX-resultظْ`SuiteOutcome` mapping, and the suite/validation-definition `run_expectations` flow ظ¤ into `datasources/gx_runner.py` (reused by the Snowflake runner and the future UC runner; no duplication). Flat-file IO (`download_bytes` + full-file `read_dataframe`) moved to `flatfile.py` behind primitives (raw config + resolved secret, not the ORM ظ¤ matches `build_snowflake_runner`), and the column profiler now shares it. Because GX runs **in-process on the DataFrame**, the run path is fully unit-tested with a canned frame (real GX execution, not mocked) ظ¤ only the network download is the deferred-smoke seam. 16 new tests; `gx_runner` 100%, `flatfile` 76% (only `download_bytes` uncovered). _Connection-type ظْ runner dispatch (Snowflake vs flat-file) is the Week-5 `run_suite` wiring; **freshness-by-filename** is the reserved `freshness` monitor kind (deferred, ADR 0012), not a GX expectation._
154	- [x] ظ£à Batch resolution ظ¤ resolve batching regex to matched files, pick latest or specific batch ظ¤ `resolve_batch` (`datasources/flatfile.py`): a batch `pattern` is a regex whose **first capture group is the batch key**; `latest` selects the greatest key (lexicographic ظ¤ ISO dates sort chronologically), falling back to the storage modified-time when the pattern has no group; `specific` selects the file whose key equals a given `batch`. `BatchNotFoundError` on no match / unknown batch. The filter+select logic is pure and fully unit-tested (8 tests); object listing (`list_files` ظ¤ S3 `list_objects_v2` paginated / ADLS `list_blobs`) is the live seam, and `resolve_batch_file` composes list+resolve. _Wiring a check's target to a resolved batch path is the Week-5 `run_suite` dispatch._
155	
156	### Unity Catalog check specifics (2 tasks ظ¤ 2/2 ظ£à)
157	- [x] ظ£à UC table check path ظ¤ UC table ظْ GX DataFrame datasource ظْ run suite ظ¤ `UnityCatalogCheckRunner` (`datasources/unity_catalog.py`): reads the target table from the Databricks SQL Warehouse into a pandas DataFrame (`pd.read_sql_table` via the databricks SQLAlchemy dialect ظ¤ reflection quotes identifiers, so no hand-built SQL) and runs the suite's GX expectations against GX's **pandas DataFrame asset** ظ¤ the "GX DataFrame datasource" shape (┬د5), the same shape Databricks Labs DQX consumes, so v1.1 swaps GXظْDQX behind this one interface. Reuses the shared `gx_runner` (`run_expectations` + result mapping) exactly like the flat-file runner; the `catalog` is pinned in the connection URL (`build_databricks_url`, now shared with the column profiler ظ¤ no duplication) so the 2-level `schema.table` resolves to `catalog.schema.table`. Because GX runs **in-process on the DataFrame**, the run path is unit-tested with a canned frame (real GX execution); only the reflect+read (`_read_table`) is the deferred-smoke seam. `build_unity_catalog_runner` mirrors `build_snowflake_runner` (raw config + resolved PAT, not the ORM). 14 tests; `unity_catalog` 91% (only `_read_table` uncovered). _Connection-type ظْ runner dispatch is the Week-5 `run_suite` wiring._
158	- [x] ظ£à Integration tests across all three datasource types ظ¤ `backend/tests/integration/test_datasource_runs.py`: end-to-end suite runs through the **real** `run_service.execute_run` + **real Postgres** persistence, with **real GX execution** for the flat-file (`FlatFileCheckRunner`) and Unity Catalog (`UnityCatalogCheckRunner`) paths on an in-memory DataFrame (only the file download / table read is stubbed) ظ¤ exercising the whole chain: `check.kind` dispatch ظْ runner ظْ GX validation ظْ severity derivation ظْ `Result` rows. Snowflake runs through a canned outcome (live warehouse is the deferred-smoke seam; its GX mapping is covered in `datasources/test_snowflake.py`). Asserts run `succeeded`, both Results persisted, severity derived (null-check ظْ `fail`, row-count ظْ `pass` with observed value). Plus a parametrized **adversarial-frame** run (numpy + pyarrow hostile columns via the shared battery) proving a column-agnostic monitor still runs + persists over a file whose data column is hostile. 7 tests.
159	
160	**Week 3 total: 18 / 18 ظ£à ظ¤ COMPLETE**

167	
168	### Frontend tooling coordinated bumps (added ظ¤ not in original roadmap) (1 task ظ¤ 1/1 ظ£à)
169	- [x] ظ£à Vite 8 coordinated bump ظ¤ `vite` ^6ظْ^8.0.16 + `@vitejs/plugin-react` ^5ظْ^6.0.2 + `vitest` ^3ظْ^4.1.8 + `@vitest/coverage-v8` ^4.1.8 in lockstep ظ¤ [PR #119](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/119) (Fixes [#65](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/65); supersedes Dependabot #111 + closed [#57](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/57)). plugin-react v6 peers `vite ^8`, vitest v4 peers `vite ^6||^7||^8` ظ¤ done early to drain the frontend dep backlog; format/lint/typecheck/test/build all green

185	- [x] ظ£à Suite list + detail two-panel layout, environment badge on each suite ظ¤ selectable suites list ظظْ detail (connection chip + env tag + checks) ظ¤ [PR #200](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/200)
186	- [x] ظ£à Form-based check editor ظ¤ catalog-driven expectation picker + dynamic typed config fields + create/edit/delete ظ¤ `expectationCatalog.ts` + `CheckDrawer` ([PR #203](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/203)). GX expectations are datasource-agnostic in v1, so one catalog serves all four types
187	- [x] ظ£à Flat file check editor ظ¤ file path + format selector ظ¤ the datasource-shaped **run-target editor** on the suite drawer ([PR #246](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/246)) authors a flat-file target (`path` + optional CSV/Parquet `file_format`), so ADLS/S3 suites become runnable; the generic catalog editor ([PR #203](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/203)) already covers flat-file expectations. _(Container/regex **batch-resolution** inputs remain a post-v1 polish ظ¤ needs the flat-file listing surface.)_
188	- [x] ظ£à Unity Catalog check editor ظ¤ catalog / schema / table inputs ظ¤ the run-target editor ([PR #246](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/246)) authors a UC target (`catalog` + `table` + optional `schema`), making UC suites runnable; expectations covered by the generic catalog editor. _(A **cascading enumerated** 3-level picker is optional polish ظ¤ blocked on a Databricks catalog-listing API.)_
189	- [x] ظ£à **Monaco SQL editor for custom check** ظ¤ a "Custom SQL" category authoring a GX `UnexpectedRowsExpectation` (ADR 0019): a lazy-loaded, locally-bundled Monaco editor (`SqlEditorField`, its own `monaco` Vite chunk so the eager bundle is untouched) + a client-side mirror of the backend read-only/single-statement guardrail (`customSql.ts`) for inline feedback. Gated to SQL datasources (Snowflake / Unity Catalog) in both the create page and the edit drawer via `expectationsByCategoryFor(connectionType)`. _(backend unblocked by ADR 0019 / [PR #258](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/258); Snowflake keyword autocomplete deferred ظ¤ Monaco ships SQL syntax highlighting only)_
190	- [x] ظ£à Column profiler panel ظ¤ inline in check editor, loads on table / file selection ظ¤ `ColumnProfilePanel` (shared by the create page `CheckNew` + the edit drawer `CheckDrawer`): a collapsed-by-default panel that profiles one column of the suite's run target (#215) via `POST /suites/{id}/profile` (no persistence) and renders null count/fraction, distinct count, min/max, and top values ظ¤ so the author can ground a range / allowed-set / null threshold in real data before saving. The profiled column **pre-fills from the check's `column` config field** (render-phase prop-sync, like `DryRunPreview`) but stays editable to explore neighbouring columns; `table`/`schema`/`catalog` (SQL) or `path`/`file_format` (flat file) are pulled from the suite target. Disabled with a reason until the suite has a table/file target + a column is entered; backend limits (no credential / unreachable / unsupported type) surface as a clean error alert. 4 vitest (disabled-states / pre-filled-profile with request-shape assertion / error). _(PR-D2; `ColumnProfilePanel.tsx` + `api/suites.ts` `profileColumns`)_

Reuses `buildCheckPayload` (so the preview runs exactly the saved check's config/thresholds) + the severity colour map; `table`/`schema` come from the suite's run target (#215, fetched via new `getSuite`). Disabled with a reason until an expectation is picked + the suite has a table target; backend limits (Snowflake-only, no-credential/unreachable) surface as a clean error alert (dev fail-softs to a 502 with fake creds). 4 vitest (disabled-states / success-outcome with request-shape assertion / error) + an e2e button-enabled assertion. _(PR-D1; `DryRunPreview.tsx` + `api/suites.ts` `getSuite`/`dryRunCheck`)_

The **History** button on `CheckDrawer` opens `CheckHistoryDrawer` ظ¤ newest-first list, latest tagged **Current**, each row showing expectation label + pretty-printed config + `Warn ظëح/Fail ظëح/Critical ظëح` thresholds + author/timestamp. View-only; **restore deferred to [#283](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/283)**. Backend migration verified up/down; 7 backend + 6 frontend tests. _(#280; backend [PR #281](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/281) + frontend [PR #282](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/282); ADR 0019 custom-SQL configs stay readable via pretty-print)_
193	- [x] ظ£à Severity tier toggle in check editor ظ¤ three-threshold UI ظ¤ optional warn/fail/critical inputs banding the unexpected-% (ADR 0016), blank = binary ظ¤ [PR #203](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/203)

195	### Access & admin UI (3 tasks ظ¤ 3/3 ظ£à)
196	- [x] ظ£à Suite sharing panel ظ¤ add / remove users, assign roles inline ظ¤ **Share** button on the suite detail opens `SharePanel` (drawer): lists collaborators by name (from the enriched `ShareRead`) and, for owner/admin, adds/changes/removes them. Add picker is a debounced `GET /users/search` type-ahead filtering out the owner + already-shared users; permission is the view/edit/admin ladder. Gated on **`SuiteRead.my_permission`** ظ¤ a new field stamping the caller's effective level (owner/admin/edit/view), populated on create/get/update + batched across the list via `suite_authz.effective_permissions` (one shares `IN`-query, no N+1) ظ¤ so a non-owner admin gets the manage controls and a viewer a read-only list. Backend enablers landed first: **PR-E2** (`GET /users/search` behind a FastAPI-free `user_service` ظ¤ case-insensitive email/display_name substring, 2-char min, limitظëج50, LIKE-escaped ظ¤ + `ShareRead` enriched with `email`/`display_name` via a joined read-only `Share.user`). 6 component tests (list-by-name, empty, searchظْgrant with owner/existing filtered, change, revoke, read-only non-admin) + backend `my_permission` + `/users/search` tests. _(PR-E2 + PR-E3)_
197	- [x] ظ£à Admin page ظ¤ list all suites, all users, access overview ظ¤ **workspace-admin control centre** shipped (backend + frontend, #289, closes the long-standing backend block). New **workspace-admin** authz primitive (config `WORKSPACE_ADMIN_EMAILS` allowlist, case-insensitive on `user.email` ظ¤ generic identity axis, no Azure/Entra claim read; **no migration**) distinct from the per-suite `view < edit < admin < owner` ladder. Backend: `admin_service` (`list_all_suites` / `list_all_users` / `list_all_access`, deliberately **unscoped** ظ¤ bypasses the owned-or-shared scoping `list_suites` applies) + `GET /admin/{suites,users,access}` gated by `require_workspace_admin` (real **403**); `/me` now exposes `is_workspace_admin`. Frontend: `/admin` page (Suites ┬╖ Users ┬╖ Access tabs) + `Forbidden` 403 page + admin-only nav item, gated on `/me`'s server-driven flag via a shared `MeProvider`/`useMe` (one `/me` fetch, reused by Profile; resets on sign-out). 23 tests (backend: scoping-bypass, counts, case-insensitive, non-admin 403, `/me` flag; frontend: forbidden gate, all three tabs, load error, MeProvider sign-out reset). _(This pulled the Week-7 prototype-adoption tasks "workspace-admin list-all endpoint" + "Admin control centre page" forward.)_
198	- [x] ظ£à Suite export / import UI (download JSON, upload JSON) ظ¤ **Export** button on the suite detail header downloads `<suite>.json` from `GET /suites/{id}/export` via a transient-anchor blob (`utils/download.ts` ظ¤ `downloadJson` + `toFilenameStem`); **Import** button on the suites header opens `ImportSuiteDrawer` ظ¤ drag/drop a `.json`, client-side parse + shape-check (fail-fast on non-suite/malformed JSON), pick a target connection, `POST /suites/import`. The parsed document is handed back **untouched** so thresholds round-trip exactly (string-or-number Decimal encoding never coerced). 11 tests (download lib: stem slug + blob/anchor/revoke; drawer: validظْimport-with-faithful-document, non-suite/malformed-JSON rejection, connection-gated Import). _(PR-E1; backend ready from Week 3)_

201	> Model the UI on [GX Cloud](https://greatexpectations.io/gx-cloud/): dedicated **full-page create flows** that **classify** what you're creating, keeping the lighter drawers for *edit*. Plan: `~/.claude/plans/fancy-moseying-raccoon.md`. Decided: in-app results page (Grafana deferred to an optional post-v1 ops add-on, never the per-suite authz path ظ¤ ADR 0018 pending); full-page create + drawer edit; connections/checks redesign now (backends ready), results after the Week-5 run-enablement backend.
202	- [x] ظ£à **Connections ظ¤ dedicated, classified add page** ظ¤ `/connections/new` splits the type picker into **Data sources** vs **Orchestration** sections (CLAUDE.md ┬د4), then the spec-driven form; the list page is grouped the same way. `CONNECTION_KIND` is the single source for the split. Edit/re-auth/delete stay on the drawer. _(PR-A1; `ConnectionNew.tsx` + sectioned `Connections.tsx`)_
203	- [x] ظ£à **Connections ظ¤ `ConnectionDrawer` trimmed to edit-only** ظ¤ create now lives on the page; the drawer drops the type/env Selects + secret + create branch, shows type/env read-only, and the list owns a simpler `editing: Connection | null` state. _(PR-A2)_
204	- [x] ظ£à **Checks ظ¤ suite selection as a route + categorized expectation picker** ظ¤ `/suites/:suiteId` makes the selected suite deep-linkable and survives the check-editor round-trip; the catalog gains a `category` (Column values / Table shape) and the picker groups by it (antd optgroups). Reserved Freshness/Volume/Schema-drift categories (ADR 0012) land with the dedicated page. _(PR-B1)_
205	- [x] ظ£à **Checks ظ¤ dedicated `/suites/:suiteId/checks/new` page** ظ¤ category ظْ expectation ظْ config/thresholds, with reserved monitor-kind categories (Freshness/Volume/Schema-drift, ADR 0012) shown disabled. Shared form logic extracted to `checkForm.ts` (conversions) + `checkFormFields.tsx` (field components), reused by the page and the now edit-only `CheckDrawer`. The natural home for the [#215](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/215) target table/path field. _(PR-B2; `CheckNew.tsx`)_
206	
207	**Week 4 total: 26 / 26 ظ£à ظ¤ complete** _(connection manager UI complete (6/6); check editor core shipped + **dry-run preview** (PR-D1) + **column profiler panel** (PR-D2) + the datasource-shaped **run-target editor** (PR #246 ظ¤ closes the flat-file + Unity Catalog target inputs, making suites runnable); **check version-history drawer** (#280; backend #281 + frontend #282 ظ¤ view-only, restore deferred to #283); **suite export/import UI** (PR-E1); **suite sharing panel** (PR-E2 backend + PR-E3 UI, gated on `SuiteRead.my_permission`); **GX-Cloud redesign complete (4/4)** ظ¤ classified connection + check create pages, edit-only drawers, suite-route/categorized picker (PR-A1/A2/B1/B2); PR-3c polish 3/3 (Settings `extra="forbid"` shipped via the `.env`/`.env.app` split, #209); **Monaco custom-SQL editor** (ADR 0019 ظ¤ custom-SQL as a GX `UnexpectedRowsExpectation`; backend #258 + frontend #259); **admin control centre** (#289 ظ¤ workspace-admin authz + `/admin/{suites,users,access}` + the `/admin` page & 403, pulling the Week-7 prototype-adoption admin tasks forward))_
208	
209	---
210	
211	## Week 5 ظ¤ Execution engine + scheduling
212	
213	**Exit gate:** Async runs with live progress across all datasource types; scheduling operational.
214	
215	### Async execution backend (10 tasks ظ¤ 10/10 ظ£à)
216	- [x] ظ£à Celery + Redis background task runner for GX scan execution ظ¤ `run_suite` task + `run_service` ظ¤ landed early via [PR 4a](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/74) + [PR 4c-i](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/78) + [PR 4c-ii](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/79)
217	- [x] ظ£à Generalise `run_suite` worker dispatch ظ¤ select the `CheckRunner` by `connection.type` via the runner registry (`build_check_runner`), replacing the Snowflake-hardcoded wiring in `worker/tasks.py`; the seam that makes the flat-file / UC run paths route correctly and post-v1 RDBMS adapters a drop-in ظ¤ [PR #146-fix](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/146) _(added per [ADR 0011](adr/0011-extensibility-seams-for-deferred-integrations.md); profiler dispatch collapsed onto a parallel registry in the same effort, [#147](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/147))_
218	- [x] ظ£à **Check target table/path** ظ¤ modeled as a **per-suite** datasource-shaped `Suite.target` (JSONB, shaped like the column-profiler request: `table`/`schema`/`catalog`/`path`/`file_format`), since `execute_run` runs all of a suite's checks against one batch (the GX data-asset-per-suite shape). `services/run_target.resolve_target` resolves it per connection type to the runner's `(table, schema, catalog)` triple (flat-file path rides the table-shaped `CheckRunner`'s `table` slot); `validate_target` is the write-time 422 guard on suite create/update. Migration `b1f2c3d4e5a6` (additive nullable column, tested up/down on Postgres). **Dispatch ungated**: `_trigger_suites` now hands each created run to `run_suite.delay` (lazy import ظ¤ workerظ¤service cycle), broker-fail marks the run `failed`; `run_suite` resolves the target from the suite (targetless ظْ clean `failed`), so the worker no longer takes a `table` arg; stale `until_week3` wording removed. 13 resolver units + worker/orchestration/suite-API target tests. _(Re-tracked from Week 3; [#215](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/215); PR-C0a)_

# DataQ v1 ظ¤ Progress tracker

> Mirrors [context/DataQ_platform_roadmap.md](../context/DataQ_platform_roadmap.md) (the 100-task roadmap) with execution status.
> **Updated at the end of every PR** ظ¤ the PR template has a checkbox to enforce.
> Source of truth for "what's done vs. what's left." CLAUDE.md ┬د13 carries only the headline.

## Status legend

| Symbol | Meaning |
|---|---|
| ظ£à | Done ظ¤ PR merged to `main` |
| ≡ااة | In progress ظ¤ open PR or partially shipped |
| ظش£ | Not started |
| ≡ا¤╡ | Deferred / scope-changed (with note) |

---

## Snapshot

| | |
|---|---|
| **Active since** | 2026-05-24 |
| **Current week** | **Week 6 of 8 (Results dashboard + alerting) ظ¤ COMPLETE (27/27); entering Week 7**. Weeks 1ظô5 complete. Close-out: the **alerting backend track** (#366 seam ┬╖ #367 Teams card ┬╖ #368 routing ┬╖ #369 dedup ┬╖ #370 suppression ┬╖ #373 per-suite config) + **prototype Phase 5ظô6** (#374 Profile ┬╖ #375 Settings ┬╖ #376 Admin reconcile ┬╖ #377 error pages) + the **notification config UI** (#378), plus redacted sample rows (#365, closed #226). **Week-7 early-credit:** Azure deploy scaffolding (#379). All Week-6 work **merged to `main`** as the stacked PR chain #366ظْ#379; follow-ups #380 (W6 close-out docs), #381 (deploy migration-gate + doc reconcile), #390 (live run-progress histogram fix, closes #316) merged after. |

## Week 2 ظ¤ Connection manager ظ¤ all datasource types (backend)

**Exit gate:** All connection types configurable and testable via API; credentials stored in Key Vault.

> **Auth-boundary discipline (per [ADR 0010](adr/0010-provider-agnostic-infrastructure-seams.md)):** new connection-CRUD endpoints (and every protected route from here on) depend on a generic internal "current user" dependency that returns DataQ's own `User` ظ¤ they must NOT read MSAL token claims directly in route/service code. Cheap now; expensive to retrofit once dozens of endpoints exist. No new abstraction layer required, just the boundary.

### Snowflake & ADF (4 tasks ظ¤ 4/4)
- [x] ظ£à API: CRUD for Snowflake connections (DEV / QA / UAT), connection test endpoint ظ¤ [PR 5](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/85) _(also introduced the `ConnectionAdapter` seam + registry per [ADR 0011](adr/0011-extensibility-seams-for-deferred-integrations.md), and `SecretStore.set` write-through ظ¤ so PRs 6-8 are pure adapter additions)_
- [x] ظ£à API: CRUD for ADF connections (subscription ID + service principal) ظ¤ PR 6 _(`ADFConnectionAdapter` in the new `orchestration/` package ظ¤ NOT `datasources/`, per CLAUDE.md ┬د4; `test()` does SP token + factory GET via httpx. Enforces `(type, env)` uniqueness for orchestrator rows via a **partial unique index** `WHERE type IN ('adf','airflow')` per [#72](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/72) / ADR 0004 ظ¤ datasources excluded, so Snowflake stays many-per-env. CRUD/API reused unchanged: pure adapter + registry + migration addition.)_
- [x] ظ£à Connection re-auth endpoint ظ¤ refresh expired Key Vault token ظ¤ `POST /connections/{id}/reauth` (`svc.reauth_connection`): rotates the credential through `SecretStore.set` **and** verifies it via the same adapter probe as `/test`, in one step (the gap PATCH+`/test` leave open). Rotation persists before the probe, so a bad new credential surfaces as 502 `connection_test_failed`; a store-write failure is 502 `connection_secret_write_failed` with the old credential untouched. 6 TestClient tests (rotate+verify ok, failed-verify-but-rotation-persists, write-fail 502, 404, secret-required 422). Type-agnostic ظ¤ applies to all six connection types
- [x] ظ£à Review `connections.secret_ref` nullability ظ¤ decide based on Airflow basic-poll / unauthenticated S3 cases ([PR #41 nit](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/41)) ظ¤ **decision: keep nullable.** It's NULL for the transient flushظْsecret-write window (create), for credential-less auth (managed-identity/IAM-role, W7, ADR 0010/0011), and for unauthenticated sources. v1 types are all secret-bearing, but presence is enforced in the **service layer** (`test_connection` ظْ 502 without a credential), not the schema ظ¤ so W7 credential-less modes need no later migration. Recorded as a comment on the `secret_ref` column in `db/models.py`

### Severity threshold tiers (warn / fail / critical) (4 tasks ظ¤ 4/4 ظ£à)
> **Day 1 design decision: severity weights ظ¤ ظ£à settled in [ADR 0005](adr/0005-severity-tier-weights.md) (warn 0.5 / fail 1.0 / critical 2.0; health = 100├ù(1ظêْ╬ثpenalty/(N├ù2.0))).**

Reads-only, persists nothing: one aggregate query (row count + null/distinct/min/max per column) + one top-N-values query per column, then `assemble_profile`. **SQL-injection-safe** ظ¤ queries are built with the **SQLAlchemy Core expression language** (`select`/`table`/`column`, dialect-quoted) so there's no raw-string SQL sink (no S608/B608/CodeQL `py/sql-injection`); identifiers are additionally allowlist-validated (`validate_identifier`, strict `^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_$]*$`) as defence-in-depth + a clean early 422, and `top_n` is `int()`-coerced. v1 ظْ 422: non-Snowflake type (dispatch generalises Week 5), bad identifier, no schema; execution failure ظْ 502 (adapter exception never echoed). min/max/top-values NaN-sanitised. 28 tests (22 pure: identifier allowlist incl. injection strings, compiled-SQL builders, assembly, div-by-zero, NaN; 6 endpoint via fake conn: stats, injection-422, unsupported-type-422, 502, edit-gated, no-schema-422); `suites.py` 100%, `profile_service` 90% (live `_open_connection` is the deferred warehouse seam)

Same `POST /suites/{id}/profile` endpoint, now polymorphic: SQL targets pass `table`/`schema`, flat-file targets pass `path` (+ optional `file_format`, else inferred from extension); response carries whichever identity applies. CSV + Parquet. **pandas "load less data" levers** (per the user's scale.html prompt): column projection (CSV `usecols`, Parquet `columns=` via pyarrow schema) so profiling 3 of N columns doesn't read all N, + row sampling (`_SAMPLE_ROWS`), + **zero-copy Arrow-backed dtypes** on the Parquet read (`dtype_backend="pyarrow"` ظ¤ parquet is already Arrow, kept zero-copy; the stat helpers + `_to_native` are Arrow-scalar/`pd.NA`-safe). Stats are vectorised pandas (no Python row loops ظ¤ enhancingperf N/A; CoW is a no-op for a read-only profiler + a process-global flag, so not toggled; pyarrow CSV engine rejected ظ¤ it's incompatible with `nrows`/callable-`usecols`). NaN/TimestampظْJSON-safe. v1 ظْ 422: unsupported type (UC still pending), missing target, unknown format, missing column; read failure ظْ 502 (exception never echoed). Reuses `ColumnProfile`/`ProfileResult`. 24 new tests (pure `profile_dataframe`/`infer_file_format`/`_to_native`/projection-parse + endpoint via fake `_read_dataframe`); `suites.py` 100%, `profile_service` 85% (only the live `_open_connection`/`_download_bytes` network seams uncovered ظ¤ deferred smoke). _Deferred: streaming/range reads (whole object still downloaded before sampling) + out-of-core (Dask) ظ¤ future work if profiling cost bites._

Injection-safe: every part (catalog/schema/table/column) is allowlist-validated (no raw-string SQL, no CodeQL py/sql-injection surface). ## 2a. Recurring cadence ظ¤ incremental loads (the "source keeps producing" clock)

> The ┬د2 generator is **one-shot** (backfills N snapshots in a single run). Freshness/
> volume/anomaly monitors only mean something if data keeps *arriving* on a cadence.
> **Three clocks ظ¤ do not conflate:** (1) **data-arrival** (source produces new data ظ¤
> NEW, this section); (2) **load+trigger** (ADF/Airflow picks up the file ظْ triggers
> DataQ ظ¤ ┬د3); (3) **check-run** (DataQ's W5 `schedules` cron, or driven by clock 2).
>
> **Decisions (2026-06-26) ظ¤ two layers, don't collapse them:**
> - **File creation (clock 1, source-system simulator) = Container Apps Job on a cron schedule.**
>   Runs the `mockdata` image (`tick ظْ write files to ADLS landing zone`). Chosen over a
>   Function App (pandas/pyarrow is a heavy *batch*, not lightweight event glue ظ¤ Functions
>   add packaging/cold-start friction) and over OS cron; reuses DataQ's Container Apps platform
>   + image, cron built in, pay-per-run. **This is *upstream* of the orchestrator** (source drops
>   files), which is cleaner/more realistic than the orchestrator also producing.
> - **Load + trigger (clock 2) = ADF/Airflow** load to Snowflake/UC ظْ on-success trigger DataQ
>   (the path under test). **Deliberately mix two trigger modalities ظ¤ not all event-driven:**
>     - **Event / file-landing** (ADF Storage-Event trigger / Airflow file-sensor or dataset DAG) ظ¤
>       fires when the Job drops a file. Use for high-frequency/latency-sensitive feeds (e.g. orders).
>     - **Schedule / time** (ADF Schedule or tumbling-window trigger / Airflow cron `schedule`) ظ¤
>       fires on a clock, processes whatever landed in the window. Use for daily/batch feeds
>       (e.g. inventory, feedback). **Set the load schedule to fire *after* the file-creation Job**
>       (e.g. Job drops inventory 01:00 ظْ scheduled load 01:15) so there's data to pick up.
>     - _Why both:_ exercises both ADF/Airflow trigger types **and** DataQ's pipeline-run monitoring +
>       10-min polling / gap-recovery fallback (the poll only meaningfully bites on scheduled pipelines).
>   Flow C (flat-file in place) has no load step ظ¤ the Job's file drop *is* the arrival; DataQ reads
>   in place (its own W5 schedule or arrival event).
>   Proposed split (adjustable): **orders ظْ event**, **tracking ظْ event**, **inventory ظْ schedule**,
>   **feedback ظْ schedule** ظ¤ one event + one schedule per warehouse target (Snowflake & UC) for parity.
> - **Required by the Job runtime:** Container Apps Jobs are **ephemeral** ظْ the generator's

- [x] ظ£à **History/audit strategy (ADR)** ظ¤ decided in [ADR 0020](adr/0020-history-and-audit-strategy.md): per-entity **Type-4** snapshot tables where config history is needed (no SCD-2; credentials never snapshotted; cascade-delete accepted; soft-delete + cross-entity audit log deferred). **First implementation: `connection_versions`** (connection name/config history, snapshot on create + name/config update; secret-only rotation records nothing). [#310](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/310) _(audit-log + soft-delete remain deferred there)_

### Prototype adoption ظ¤ final polish passes (added ظ¤ not in original roadmap) (4 tasks ظ¤ 0/4) ظ¤ tracked in epic [#176](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/176)
> Closes out the prototype after the Week-6 structural pass (the full screen set ظ¤ incl. **Settings** ظ¤ now lands in Week 6, ADR [0022](adr/0022-week6-prototype-adoption-and-chart-library.md)): what remains here is the **KPI honesty pass** + the final **visual-fidelity pass** to the design-system guidelines. Admin needed a real **workspace-admin** authz concept (today `admin` is per-suite only) ظ¤ the prototype's client-side "View as" switch is **not** used (real authz is server-side, CLAUDE.md ┬د11); that backend + page shipped early via #289. **To open the prototype, see the *Design source* note in the Week-6 prototype-adoption group** (project `317fec67-2b8a-498c-8f6b-523750916a8d` via the `DesignSync` tool).
- [x] ظ£à **Backend: workspace-admin list-all endpoint** ظ¤ **shipped early in Week 4 (#289)** to unblock the Admin page. `GET /admin/suites` ┬╖ `/admin/users` ┬╖ `/admin/access` behind a **workspace-admin** authz check (config `WORKSPACE_ADMIN_EMAILS` allowlist, distinct from the per-suite `view < edit < admin < owner` ladder; no migration). Deliberately unscoped (bypasses owned-or-shared). `/me` exposes `is_workspace_admin`.
- [x] ظ£à **Admin control centre page** (`/admin`) ظ¤ **shipped early in Week 4 (#289)**. Suites / Users / Access tabs on the new endpoint, with a **real 403** `Forbidden` page for non-admins (server-driven via `/me`'s `is_workspace_admin` ظ¤ *not* a client "View as" toggle), admin-only nav, shared `MeProvider`/`useMe`. _(Week-6 reconciles this layout to the prototype ظ¤ see that week's Admin task.)_
- [ ] ظش£ **Dashboard KPI honesty pass** ظ¤ the Dashboard renders only metrics with a real data model: pass-rate, run counts, and `metric_value`-backed trends (ADR 0012 seam). **Omit "anomalies detected"** (the `anomaly` monitor-kind is reserved, not implemented in v1) and **"avg. time to resolution"** (there is no incident/resolution model). Re-introduce when the monitor kinds / model exist (post-v1 Theme A). _Design ref: `templates/app/DashboardScreen.jsx` (the KPI card row ظ¤ keep pass-rate / runs / trends, drop the two unbacked KPIs)._

Keep using **semantic tokens** so post-v1 dark mode inherits for free. _Design ref: `guidelines/{type-body,type-mono,color-indigo,color-neutrals,color-status,spacing-scale,elevation,radius}.card.html` + `tokens/{fonts,typography,elevation,spacing,colors}.css` + `assets/fonts/`; readme ┬د"Visual foundations" / "Motion" / "Focus" / "Iconography"._

**Week 7 total: 2 / 33** _(+4 prototype-adoption tasks: workspace-admin endpoint ظ£à + Admin page ظ£à both shipped early in Week 4 via #289; remaining: Dashboard KPI honesty pass, visual-fidelity pass. **Settings page moved to Week 6** with the full-screen-set adoption, ADR 0022.)_

**Not adopted (intentional, per design review):** the prototype's **"View as" role switch** (real authz is server-side ظ¤ Azure AD + per-suite sharing; CLAUDE.md ┬د11), its **framework-free component code + `_ds_bundle.js` / `window.*` globals** (production uses real antd per the design README), and the **teal "Enterprise Monitor" palette** (the shipped indigo theme stands; the design system itself already superseded the teal `uploads/DESIGN.md` direction).

## How to update this file

When merging a PR:

1. Find the task(s) it implements in the relevant week.
2. Flip `ظش£` ظْ `ظ£à` (or `ظش£` ظْ `≡ااة` if partial).
3. Append the PR link: `ظ¤ [PR #N](https://github.com/.../pull/N)`.
4. Update the per-week subtotal at the bottom of the week.
5. Update the **Snapshot** table at the top (task count, open PRs/issues) and the **Aggregate** table.
6. If the PR added an out-of-roadmap task (e.g. ADR-driven scope change), add a row with the note.

PR-template checkbox enforces this. If the change is purely tooling / docs that doesn't map to a roadmap task, tick the "N/A" checkbox.

=== EPIC #176 ===
Tracking epic for **Week 7 ظ¤ Deployment, hardening & docs**. Mirrors the roadmap; the task-level source of truth remains [docs/progress.md](docs/progress.md). Milestone: `Week 7 ظ¤ Deployment, hardening & docs`.


**DevOps & deployment**
- [ ] Containerise FastAPI + React + Celery + Redis
- [ ] Push images to Azure Container Registry
- [ ] Deploy to Azure Container Apps (API + Celery worker) + Azure Static Web App (React UI)
- [ ] CI/CD pipeline ظ¤ lint, test, build, deploy on merge to `main`
- [ ] Application Insights integration ظ¤ traces, errors, slow queries, Celery task metrics

**Azure Monitor webhook setup (one-time infra, post-deployment)**
- [ ] Create Action Group (pre-prod) ظ¤ type: Webhook, URL: `https://<pre-prod-api>.azurecontainerapps.io/api/v1/adf/events`, shared secret: value from Key Vault
- [ ] Create Alert Rule (pre-prod) ظ¤ scope: `example-adf-preprod` factory, signal: `Failed pipeline runs`, dimension: all pipelines (tick "include all future values"), action: Action Group above
- [ ] Create Action Group (prod) ظ¤ same config pointing to prod API URL
- [ ] Create Alert Rule (prod) ظ¤ scope: `example-adf-prod` factory, same signal + dimension config
- [ ] Smoke test ظ¤ trigger a deliberate pipeline failure in DEV, confirm webhook fires, DB updates, and ADF panel reflects failure within 5 min

**FastMCP ظ¤ MCP server**
- [ ] Install FastMCP, scaffold `mcp_server.py`, mount MCP app into FastAPI at `/mcp` ظ¤ `app.mount("/mcp", mcp.get_asgi_app())`
- [ ] Wire Azure AD token validation into FastMCP's auth provider ظ¤ validate Bearer token on every MCP tool call using the same `verify_azure_ad_token` logic as the REST API
- [ ] Implement `list_suites` resource ظ¤ all suites accessible to current user, name / datasource / env / last run status / check count
- [ ] Implement `get_suite_results` resource ظ¤ latest DQ run results for a suite; pass/fail per check, observed vs expected values, sample failing rows
- [ ] Implement `get_health_score` resource ظ¤ overall health score and 7-day trend, filterable by env and datasource type
- [ ] Implement `get_adf_pipeline_status` resource ظ¤ latest ADF pipeline run status with correlated DQ result per pipeline
- [ ] Implement `trigger_suite_run` tool ظ¤ async GX suite execution against a given datasource + env; returns `run_id` for polling
- [ ] Implement `get_run_status` tool ظ¤ poll live check-by-check progress for a running execution using `run_id`
- [ ] Implement `create_check` tool ظ¤ add a new GX expectation to a suite (form path: check type + column + threshold; or raw SQL)
- [ ] Implement `profile_column` tool ظ¤ run column profiler on a table or file; returns nulls, distinct count, min/max, top values
- [ ] Write LLM-optimised docstrings for all 8 tools ظ¤ describe what the tool does, when to use it, what parameters mean, and what the response contains; tested against realistic natural language queries
- [ ] Test end-to-end with Claude Desktop ظ¤ verify tool selection is correct for: "what failed today?", "run the orders suite on DEV", "why did the customer pipeline fail?", "add a null check on email"
- [ ] Document MCP connection config for Claude Desktop, Claude.ai, GitHub Copilot (`mcp.json`), and Cursor (`~/.cursor/mcp.json`) in README

**Hardening & docs**
- [ ] E2E test coverage for critical paths (auth, Snowflake run, flat file run, UC run, results)
- [ ] Error handling audit ظ¤ all API endpoints return consistent error shapes
- [ ] Ensure all FastAPI endpoints have `summary`, `description`, `tags`, and `response_model` for clean Swagger output
- [ ] README + deployment guide + environment variable reference
- [ ] Team onboarding session + feedback collection


**Prototype adoption ظ¤ final polish passes (added ظ¤ not in original roadmap)**
> DataQ Design System prototype; closes out the prototype after the Week-6 structural pass (the full screen set ظ¤ incl. **Settings** ظ¤ now lands in Week 6, ADR [0022](../blob/main/docs/adr/0022-week6-prototype-adoption-and-chart-library.md)). What remains here is the KPI honesty + visual-fidelity passes. Mirrors the matching group in [docs/progress.md](docs/progress.md).
- [x] Backend: workspace-admin list-all endpoint (`GET /admin/suites` ┬╖ `/users` ┬╖ `/access`) ظ¤ **shipped early in Week 4 (#289)**
- [x] Admin control centre page (`/admin`, `AdminScreen`) ظ¤ real server-side 403 for non-admins (not a client "View as" toggle) ظ¤ **shipped early in Week 4 (#289)**; Week 6 reconciles its layout to the prototype
- [ ] Dashboard KPI honesty pass ظ¤ only real-data KPIs (omit "anomalies detected" / "avg. time to resolution")
- [ ] Visual-fidelity pass to the design spec ظ¤ Inter / JetBrains Mono fonts, motion / focus tokens


**Live test/demo-data environment (v1-supporting harness ظ¤ external, ADR [0021](../blob/main/docs/adr/0021-demo-test-data-environment-strategy.md))**
> Out-of-repo harness (Terraform / retail mock-data generators / Databricks notebook are **not** git-tracked); the mechanism that discharges the standing *"deferred live warehouse/file run smoke"*. Detailed steps live in the local `HARNESS_TODO.md`; mirrors the *Live test/demo-data environment* sub-section in [docs/progress.md](docs/progress.md).
- [ ] Terraform infra bootstrap ظ¤ ADLS / Snowflake / Unity Catalog / ADF (Airflow runs **local via docker-compose**, not Terraform-provisioned in the v1 profile; datasources point at real cloud accounts)
- [ ] Retail data model + mock-data generators (Product / Inventory / Location / Sales / Returns / Logistics / Guest + discount/promo/tax/FX/price; **seed deliberate DQ failures** so checks actually fail)
- [ ] Reference **Flow A** ظ¤ file lands in ADLS ظْ ADF/Airflow loads to Snowflake/UC ظْ DataQ checks the warehouse table
- [ ] Reference **Flow B** ظ¤ file lands in UC RAW ظْ Databricks notebook RAWظْSilverظْGold ظْ DataQ checks UC tables
- [ ] Reference **Flow C** ظ¤ file lands in ADLS, stays flat ظْ DataQ checks it in place (`FlatFileCheckRunner` + batch resolution)
- [ ] Discharge the deferred live warehouse/file run smoke once a flow runs green end-to-end (flip the ظش£ in `docs/progress.md` + note in CLAUDE.md ┬د13)

content.content:
1:- [ ] Teardown verified (`terraform destroy`) so the demo env is disposable ظ¤ **not yet run** (note: the UI-created+imported UC catalog will be destroyed by TF; the hidden westus2 Postgres name reservation is external to state)
2:- [x] **No Azure resource names leak into the DataQ repo** (ADR 0010/0013) ظ¤ names live only in the harness `terraform/` (outside the repo, gitignored)
6:## 2. Retail data model + mock-data generators (external harness)
12:- [x] **Product** ظ¤ sku_id, style_id, category, colour, material, size, unit_price, active, created_at (`products`)
13:- [x] **Inventory** ظ¤ on_hand, planned, daily snapshot with `load_ts` (`inventory_snapshot`, Parquet per date)
14:- [x] **Location** ظ¤ store, address, open/close hours (`locations`)
15:- [x] **Orders** ظ¤ `orders_header` (order_number, channel, customer, status, subtotal, tax, order_total, ts) ظ¤ `order_lines` (sku, qty, unit_price, line_total). `subtotal = sum(line_total)` tie-out shaped clean; intra-dataset reconciliation target #1
16:- [x] **Sales lifecycle** ظ¤ placed ┬╖ shipped ┬╖ audited events carrying `order_number` (`sales_events`, Flow B bronze; rolls up to gold revenue for the cross-platform reconcile)
17:- [x] **Payments / Finance** ظ¤ `payments` (method, status, captured_amount) + `refunds` (amount, reason); `order_total ظ¤ payment_captured ظ¤ refund` testbed live. **Settlement ledger now modelled** (`settlements` ظ¤ net-amount mismatch issue seeded)
18:- [x] **Return / Cancellation** ظ¤ `returns` table (order, sku, reason, channel, qty) + `cancelled` order status **and** a standalone `cancellations` entity (cancellation-consistency issue seeded)
19:- [x] **Logistics** ظ¤ shipment/tracking/carrier via `tracking_events` (stable `tracking_number` across readyظْin_transitظْdelivered batches). **Fulfilling node now modelled** (`fulfillment_nodes` + `order_fulfillment` ظ¤ node-orphan issue seeded)
20:- [x] **Channel / Marketplace** ظ¤ online ┬╖ store ┬╖ marketplace as a first-class `channels` dimension + `channel` FK on orders/returns/feedback (null-channel + orphan issues seeded)
21:- [x] **Guest / Customer** ظ¤ `customers` (name, address, **email, phone** PII for #226) + `feedback` survey (rating, comment, email). Chatbot folded into feedback
22:- [x] **Cross-cutting** ظ¤ `promotions` (type/value/coupon-style) + per-order `tax`, plus `exchange_rates` + standalone `discounts` now modelled
23:- [x] _Optional (domain realism) ظ¤ **all now built** (no longer deferred):_
43:- [x] Generator script (Python, pandas+pyarrow+stdlib ظ¤ **no Faker**, stdlib `random`+pools for referential control) emitting CSV/Parquet/NDJSON, laid out per the route map. **Emits to local `out/`** (ADLS upload is ┬د3; the folder tree is already landing-zone-shaped)
44:- [x] **Keep shared keys consistent across domains AND across batch/incremental loads** ظ¤ shared key-pool strategy in `mockdata/generate.py` (masters generated first, transactional tables draw from the pools). **Verified:**
49:- [x] **Shape ظëح1 dataset for each reserved monitor kind** (ADR 0012) ظ¤ done in the generator (built post-v1, shaped now):
53:- [x] **Seed deliberate DQ issues** ظ¤ nulls ┬╖ dupes ┬╖ FK orphans (`SKU-000000`/`CUST-000000`) ┬╖ stale freshness ┬╖ volume drop ┬╖ schema drift ┬╖ reconciliation mismatch. All recorded in `out/_dq_issues_manifest.json` with affected keys (the QA/QE agent ┬د4 asserts failures land *exactly* there)
54:- [x] Parameterise volume (`--tier S/M/L/XL`, perf ┬د6) + `--seed`; `--no-issues` for clean data; run command + full docs in `mockdata/README.md`
58:## 2a. Recurring cadence ظ¤ incremental loads (the "source keeps producing" clock)
91:- [x] **Generator `tick` mode** ظ¤ `python -m mockdata tick --feed {orders,tracking,inventory,feedback}` emits **one** batch-suffixed file per dataset (`<name>_b000123.<fmt>`, append-style), not the full backfill. `init` subcommand seeds state + emits the reference loads once. (`mockdata/increment.py` + `state.py`)
92:- [x] **Persisted state** (`state/state.json` + `state/masters/*.parquet`): master pools + climbing id counters (`order_number` never collides) + open-shipment registry (a `tracking_number` from an orders tick gets `readyظْin_transitظْdelivered` on successive tracking ticks). **Verified:** 2 order ticks ظْ ranges 1ظô40 / 41ظô80, 0 collisions; tracking# identical across the 3 lifecycle batches; cross-feed FK integrity holds; ruff+black clean
93:- [x] **Per-feed cadence map** (cron) ظ¤ documented in `mockdata/README.md`: orders `0 0,6,12,18 * * *` ┬╖ tracking `0 0,12 * * *` ┬╖ inventory `0 1 * * *` ┬╖ feedback/reference `0 1 */2 * *`; masters via `init` (rare). Anomaly flags `--volume-drop`/`--stale`/`--schema-drift` + `--issues` per tick
94:- [x] **`state.py` pluggable backend** ظ¤ `StateBackend` seam: `LocalStateBackend` (default) + `AdlsStateBackend` (Gen2 blob via `DefaultAzureCredential`, azure libs lazy-imported). `make_backend()` picks by URI (`adls://<acct>/<fs>/<prefix>` else local path). **Verified:** local tick flow intact post-refactor; ruff+black clean. ADLS impl coded, runnable against real ADLS post-apply
95:- [x] **`Dockerfile`** (`python:3.13-slim` + pandas/pyarrow/azure; `ENTRYPOINT python -m mockdata`) + `.dockerignore` (keeps secrets.sh/terraform/out/state out of context). **Verified:** image builds; in-container `init`+`tick` write the route layout; **state survives across separate container runs** (mounted vol here; ADLS backend in the Job); `azure SDK OK` in image
96:- [x] **emit output backend ظْ ADLS** ظ¤ factored the byte-storage primitive into a shared `storage.py` `Store` seam (`LocalStore` + `AdlsStore` + `make_store`, used by **both** `state.py` and `emit.py` ظ¤ one ADLS-auth impl, no dup). `emit.py` routes every write (csv/parquet/json via buffers + jsonl `append_text`) through the store; `--out adls://<acct>/<fs>[/<prefix>]` now writes the landing zone directly. **Verified:** local backfill+tick intact; ruff+black clean; in the image, `make_store` routing + lazy azure import + DataLake client construct, and a write reaches the Azure auth layer (fails only on missing identity ظ¤ managed identity supplies it in the Job). **Job is now fully standalone** (no mount needed); only live ADLS write awaits apply
97:- [x] **Container Apps Job (cron)** per feed ظ¤ **APPLIED + SEEDED**: 4 jobs (`mockdata-orders`/`-tracking`/`-inventory`/`-feedback`) provisioned on their crons, pulling `dataqacr3erlgd.azurecr.io/mockdata:latest`. **`init` run once (2026-06-27)** against `adls://dataqharness3erlgd/landing` (state at `ظخ/landing/state`, tier S seed 42) ظ¤ seeded masters + reference loads + `state.json`; a verification `tick --feed orders` emitted batch `b000001` (304 rows) cleanly, so the scheduled ticks (same command) will run. _Re-run `init` after a `terraform destroy`/recreate (state lives in the storage account)._
98:- [x] **ADF/Airflow load ظ¤ APPLIED + RUNNING LIVE**: Airflow CeleryExecutor (schedule DAGs `flow_a_snowflake_load` 9 feeds / `flow_a_uc_reference` 8 dims / `flow_b_medallion`; event DAG `flow_a_payments_event`), ADF `pl_flow_a_orders` (blob-event) + `pl_flow_a_customers` (timer). **No trigger push** ظ¤ decoupled (DataQ polls; see ┬د3a). The only remaining gate is DataQ's *own* deploy so its poller can read the ADF/Airflow REST APIs (separate deploy track). NB: the ADF blob-event trigger needed the ADF MI granted on the storage account to *fire* ظ¤ a role assignment the Contributor SP can't make (manual grant)
99:- [ ] Verify a few cadence ticks produce **monotonic** freshness/volume series in DataQ `/results` (the monitors trend, not just one green dot)
103:## 3. Reference flows (each exercises a distinct DataQ run path)
111:- [x] File lands in ADLS landing zone (from ┬د2 generator) ظ¤ via the ┬د2a mockdata cron Jobs
112:- [x] **Orchestrator picks up + loads ظ¤ RUNNING LIVE END-TO-END** ظ¤ **Airflow** (schedule): `flow_a_snowflake_load` (**9 feeds** ظْ Snowflake `write_pandas`) + `flow_a_uc_reference` (**8 reference dims** ظْ UC, databricks-sql chunked insert) + `flow_b_medallion` (bronzeظْsilverظْgold); **Airflow** (event): `flow_a_payments_event` (ADLS file-sensor ظْ Snowflake `PAYMENTS`). **ADF** (event): `pl_flow_a_orders` blob-event trigger ظْ SnowflakeV2 Copy ظْ `ORDERS_HEADER`; **ADF** (timer): `pl_flow_a_customers` schedule trigger ظْ `CUSTOMERS`. All four modalities verified loading on a clean slate
113:- [-] ~~On success, the orchestrator hits DataQ's trigger path~~ ظ¤ **REMOVED (decoupling, 2026-06-27).** No push from the orchestrator: no Airflow callback, no ADF Web-activity webhook. DataQ (one of several possible monitors) discovers runs by **polling** the ADF/Airflow REST APIs (ADR 0006/0007) ظ¤ the orchestrators stay pure ETL
114:- [~] DataQ suite runs against the loaded **warehouse table**; result row persists ظ¤ suites + trigger-bindings specced in `dataq_setup/` (`flow_a_snowflake`, `flow_a_uc_reference`); live run apply+deploy-gated
115:- [ ] Verify in `/results`: run succeeded, severity derived, metric_value populated ظ¤ apply+deploy-gated
116:- [~] **Snowflake suite** exercises a **custom-SQL** check (ADR 0019) ظ¤ `order_total <> ROUND(SUM(line_total)*1.08,2)` reconciliation specced in `dataq_setup/README.md`; create at deploy
117:- [~] **UC plain-load suite** on Location/Promotions ظ¤ `flow_a_uc_reference` suite specced in `dataq_setup/` (no Silver/Gold ظ¤ that's Flow B)
123:- [x] File lands in bronze ظ¤ generator drops `databricks_medallion/bronze/{sales_events,feedback}/*.json`; notebook reads them into UC `raw`
124:- [x] **Databricks notebook** runs bronze ظْ silver ظْ gold ظ¤ `databricks/medallion_notebook.py` (key-based ADLS via spark conf; raw load ظْ silver clean+PII-hash ظْ gold daily_revenue rollup). Submitted by `flow_b_medallion` DAG via the Databricks SDK (parses clean in the image)
125:- [~] DataQ suites on UC Silver/Gold ظ¤ `flow_b_silver`/`flow_b_gold` specced in `dataq_setup/`; live apply+deploy-gated
126:- [x] **Gold hosts the reserved freshness/volume/anomaly targets** ظ¤ `gold.daily_revenue` (daily rollup) is the shaped target
127:- [x] **Cross-platform reconciliation target** (ADR 0014) ظ¤ Snowflake `order_total` ظ¤ Databricks `gold.daily_revenue` (sales_events `placed.amount` = order_total ظْ rolls up to tie out); the `comparison` demo shaped
128:- [x] notebook completion triggers DataQ ظ¤ `flow_b_medallion` DAG `on_dataq_success` ظْ `(airflow, flow_b_medallion)` binding
129:- [ ] Verify results per layer (bronze schema-drift, silver PII-redaction, gold freshness/volume) ظ¤ apply+deploy-gated
135:- [x] File lands in ADLS and **stays a flat file** (no warehouse load) ظ¤ `adls_flatfile/{logistics_tracking,inventory_snapshot}/`, no orchestrator load step
136:- [x] DataQ flat-file connection + suite targets the path with a **batching regex** ظ¤ specced in `dataq_setup/README.md`: tracking `tracking_events_(.+)\.csv`, inventory `inventory_snapshot_(.+)\.parquet`, strategy `latest` (first capture group = batch key). No trigger-binding ظ¤ runs on DataQ's own schedule
137:- [ ] Run resolves the **latest** batch and checks it directly ظ¤ apply+deploy-gated
138:- [ ] Verify batch resolution picks the right file + result persists ظ¤ apply+deploy-gated
139:- [~] **CSV** path: tracking events ظ¤ checks specced (tracking-number/event-status + freshness) in `dataq_setup/`
140:- [~] **Parquet** path: inventory snapshot ظ¤ checks specced (volume band ┬▒20% + freshness on latest) in `dataq_setup/`
144:## 4. QA/QE agent ظ¤ module-by-module + E2E (this IS git-tracked: `.claude/agents/`)
149:- [ ] Define agent scope: per-module functional checks (connections, suites, checks, run paths, profiler, results, orchestration) + full E2E against the harness
150:- [ ] Write `.claude/agents/qa-qe.md` (tools, model, system prompt)
151:- [ ] Wire it to drive the three flows above as E2E smoke once the harness is up
152:- [ ] Decide invocation cadence (per-PR? on-demand? pre-release?)
153:- [ ] **This file is committed** ظ¤ open a PR per working agreements (branch `feature/qa-qe-agent`)
157:## 5. HashiCorp Vault ظ¤ secret-management spike (post-v1 / backlog)
161:- [ ] Stand up Vault locally (dev mode / Docker) in the harness
162:- [ ] Prototype a `VaultSecretStore` against the existing `SecretStore` interface (spike branch, not merged)
163:- [ ] Confirm no interface change needed (if it needs one, that's a finding worth an ADR note)
164:- [ ] Write up: does the seam hold? effort to make Vault a first-class option? ظْ backlog issue / ADR follow-up

content.text:
I now have the full Week 7 picture across all three sources. Before I write the plan, let me reconcile one important signal: the harness `HARNESS_TODO.md` says the external harness is **live end-to-end as of 2026-06-27** (ADF/Airflow loading Snowflake + UC + flat files, mockdata cron jobs seeded), and the *only* remaining gate is **DataQ's own cloud deploy** so its poller/check-runs can read that data. Meanwhile progress.md/CLAUDE.md still describe the deploy as blocked on Azure RP registration (Owner).

That reconciliation drives the whole sequencing, so let me confirm a couple of decisions with you before finalizing.

options.description: RP registration / Owner access not yet resolved. Plan deploy-independent work first (FastMCP, hardening, consistency fixes), with the deploy + webhook + harness live-smoke as a track that fires once it clears.

options.label: Resolved ظ¤ deploy can proceed

options.label: Unsure / external to me

=== deploy/README.md ===
# DataQ ظ¤ deployment scaffolding

Apply-ready Azure deploy scaffolding (Week 7). **Nothing here deploys on its own**
ظ¤ the workflow ([.github/workflows/deploy.yml](../.github/workflows/deploy.yml)) is
`workflow_dispatch`-only until the Azure resources exist and the secrets/vars
below are set. This documents what to provision and how the pieces fit.

Azure is **one** deploy target behind the app's seams (ADR 0010/0013) ظ¤ the
manifests here are infra config, not business logic. No Azure resource names are
hardcoded in app code; they live only as workflow `vars`/`secrets`.

## Topology

```
Browser ظ¤ظû║ Azure Static Web App (frontend/dist)
              ظ¤é  /api/* proxied to the linked backend (same-origin ظْ no CORS)
              ظû╝
        Azure Container Apps
          ظت dataq-api     (FastAPI image, external ingress :8000)
          ظت dataq-worker  (same image, `celery -A ... worker` + beat)
          ظت dataq-migrate (Container Apps Job: `alembic upgrade head`)
              ظ¤é
              ظ¤£ظ¤ظû║ Azure Database for PostgreSQL (DATABASE_URL)
              ظ¤£ظ¤ظû║ Azure Cache for Redis        (REDIS_URL)
              ظ¤£ظ¤ظû║ Azure Key Vault              (SECRET_STORE=azure_key_vault, managed identity)
              ظ¤¤ظ¤ظû║ Application Insights         (APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING)
        Azure Container Registry (ACR) ظ¤ holds the backend image
```

Both api + worker run the **same** image ([backend/Dockerfile](../backend/Dockerfile),
build context = repo root). The frontend is built and uploaded to SWA by the
workflow (the [frontend/Dockerfile](../frontend/Dockerfile) is the container
alternative ظ¤ only needed if you run the UI on Container Apps instead of SWA).

## One-time provisioning

The datasource + compute infra is stood up by the external Terraform harness
(ADR 0021) ظ¤ see `HARNESS_TODO.md`. Beyond that, this app needs:

1. **ACR** + an **ACA environment** + the three apps/job above. The api/worker run
   `uvicorn ظخ` / `celery ظخ`; the migrate **job** runs `alembic upgrade head`.
2. **Managed identity** on the api + worker apps with **Key Vault Secrets User**
   on the vault (so `DefaultAzureCredential` resolves `SECRET_STORE=azure_key_vault`).
3. **App env**: set the keys in [deploy/.env.app.prod.example](.env.app.prod.example)
   on the api + worker apps. Secret values (DB/Redis URL, App Insights) are Key
   Vault-backed Container Apps secrets ظ¤ never literals.
4. **SWA linked backend**: link the `dataq-api` Container App as the SWA backend so
   `/api/*` is proxied same-origin (then `CORS_ALLOW_ORIGINS` can stay empty). If
   instead the SPA calls the API cross-origin, set `CORS_ALLOW_ORIGINS` to the SWA
   origin (the FastAPI CORS middleware turns on only when it's non-empty).
5. **Azure Monitor ظْ ADF webhook** alert rule (Week-7 task) ظ¤ needs the deployed
   API URL; configure after the first deploy.

## GitHub config the workflow reads

Set under repo **Settings ظْ Secrets and variables ظْ Actions**, and add a
federated credential for OIDC login (subject = this repo's `production`
environment).

**Secrets:** `AZURE_CLIENT_ID`, `AZURE_TENANT_ID`, `AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID`
(OIDC app registration), `AZURE_STATIC_WEB_APPS_API_TOKEN`.

**Variables:** `ACR_NAME`, `ACR_LOGIN_SERVER`, `AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP`,
`API_APP_NAME`, `WORKER_APP_NAME`, `MIGRATE_JOB_NAME`, and the non-secret
`VITE_AZURE_*` build values.

## Going live

1. Provision the resources + set the secrets/vars above.
2. Run the **Deploy** workflow manually (`workflow_dispatch`) to validate end-to-end.
3. To deploy on every merge, uncomment the `push: branches: [main]` trigger in
   the workflow.

Migrations are additive/backward-compatible (CLAUDE.md), so the workflow runs
`alembic upgrade head` **before** rolling the apps ظ¤ the running old code
tolerates the new schema. (This is exactly the dev-DB step that, when skipped,
500s the checks endpoint after a schema-adding deploy.)

=== ls deploy fully ===
deploy/README.md
deploy/.env.app.prod.example

23:  contents: read
