29:  backend:
95:  frontend:

=== #382 body ===
Hardening of `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` (added in #379, currently `workflow_dispatch`-only) that must land **before** the automatic `push: [main]` trigger is enabled for production deploys. Surfaced in the #366ظْ#380 stacked-review (finding **F8**).

Two items:

1. **Gate the `frontend` job on `backend` success.** Today `backend` (build ظْ migrate ظْ roll API/worker) and `frontend` (build ظْ SWA deploy) run as independent jobs with no `needs:`. A backend/migration failure does not stop the frontend deploy ظْ a **partial deploy** (new UI against an un-rolled / half-migrated API). Add `needs: backend` to the `frontend` job.
2. **Pin the image to an immutable tag.** `IMAGE` defaults to `ظخ:${{ inputs.image_tag }}` (default `latest`). `latest` is mutable ظ¤ the migrate job, API, and worker could each resolve a *different* image if a push races the deploy. Pin to the commit SHA (`${{ github.sha }}`) so migrate + API + worker roll the exact same build.

Both are no-ops while the workflow is manual-only, but they are correctness/safety prerequisites for unattended `push:[main]` deploys. Non-blocking; deploy stays `workflow_dispatch`-only until then.

_Filed from the #366ظْ#380 review follow-ups._

input.content:
# Week 7 ظ¤ Deployment, hardening & docs ظ¤ execution plan

## Context

Week 6 is closed (27/27); we're entering **Week 7**, whose exit gate is *"production-ready v1
deployed to Azure, CI/CD live, team onboarded."* The roadmap counts **33 Week-7 tasks** (2 done early
via #289), tracked in [docs/progress.md](../../Coding/Python/DataQ/docs/progress.md) and epic
[#176](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/176).

Two facts shape the sequencing:

1. **The external harness is live end-to-end** (HARNESS_TODO, 2026-06-27): ADF + Airflow are loading
   Snowflake + UC + flat files on real cloud accounts, mockdata cron Jobs are seeded. The *only* gate
   left on the harness is **DataQ's own cloud deploy** so its poller/check-runs can read that data ظ¤
   i.e. the deploy is the keystone that also discharges the standing "deferred live warehouse/file run
   smoke."
2. **The cloud apply is still blocked on Azure RP registration (needs Owner).** Confirmed with the user.

So the user asked to **lead with the deploy track**, but the actual `terraform apply` / ACA roll /
webhook setup cannot run until the Owner blocker clears. This plan therefore splits the deploy track
into **Phase 1 ظ¤ deploy-ready hardening (buildable + testable now, no Azure access)** and
**Phase 2 ظ¤ cloud apply (gated on Owner/RP registration)**, then sequences the remaining tracks
(FastMCP, hardening/docs, consistency, prototype polish) after.

**Harness GitHub issue-filing is out of scope** (user decision); the harness stays external/not
git-tracked.

### What #379 already shipped (don't rebuild)
- `backend/Dockerfile` **and** `frontend/Dockerfile` + `frontend/nginx.conf` ظ¤ both images exist, so
  roadmap task "Containerise FastAPI + React + Celery + Redis" is effectively **done** (progress.md
  still marks it ≡ااة ظ¤ flip to ظ£à after verifying the frontend image builds).
- `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` ظ¤ `workflow_dispatch`-only; `backend` (buildظْmigrateظْroll) and
  `frontend` (buildظْSWA) jobs.
- `deploy/README.md` + `deploy/.env.app.prod.example` ظ¤ topology, provisioning, GitHub config.
- `backend/app/core/secrets.py::AzureKeyVaultStore` ظ¤ coded, lazy-imported, **0% test coverage**.
- `backend/app/core/logging.py:104` ظ¤ opencensus `AzureLogHandler`, connection-string-gated.
- CORS middleware in `backend/app/main.py`, `settings.environment: Literal["dev","staging","prod"]`.

---

## Track A (LEAD) ظ¤ Deployment

### Phase 1 ظ¤ deploy-ready hardening (buildable now, no Azure access). One PR per item.

**A1. CI/CD safety gate ظ¤ `deploy.yml` ([#382](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/382)).**
Prerequisite for any unattended `push:[main]` deploy. In `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`:
- Add `needs: backend` to the `frontend` job (no partial deploy of new UI against an un-rolled API).
- Pin `IMAGE` to `${{ github.sha }}` instead of mutable `latest`, so the migrate job + API + worker
  roll the *exact same* build. Keep `workflow_dispatch`-only; do **not** uncomment `push:[main]` yet.
- PR closes #382. No app code; CI-only.

**A2. Prod-docs gate ([#170](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/170)).**
`backend/app/main.py:55` constructs `FastAPI(title="DataQ API", lifespan=lifespan)` with Swagger always
on. Gate it on the existing `settings.environment`:
`docs_url=None, redoc_url=None, openapi_url=None` when `environment == "prod"`, else the defaults.
Add a test asserting the three are `None` in prod and present in dev. Closes #170.

**A3. App Insights integration ظ¤ traces/errors/slow-queries/Celery metrics.**
Extend the existing connection-string-gated seam in `core/logging.py` (do **not** add a second config
path). Per ADR 0010 (vendor-neutral, App Insights = one impl behind the seam), prefer the
**`azure-monitor-opentelemetry`** distro / OTLP so the backend stays swappable ظ¤ but keep it a **no-op
when `applicationinsights_connection_string` is empty** (local/CI unaffected). Instrument: FastAPI
request spans, Celery task spans (propagating the existing `request_id`), and the SQLAlchemy slow-query
hook. Pin the new dep in `backend/requirements.txt` (single source of truth) and confirm `pip-audit`
stays clean. Tests cover the "enabled vs no-op" branch with the SDK mocked. (No GH issue ظ¤ roadmap task.)

**A4. Real-vault integration test for `AzureKeyVaultStore` ([#56 nit / #169 sibling]).**
`AzureKeyVaultStore` (secrets.py:89) is 0% covered ظ¤ the lazy-import `get`/`set` branch. Write a unit
test that mocks `azure.identity.DefaultAzureCredential` + `azure.keyvault.secrets.SecretClient` and
asserts `get`/`set` round-trip through the client and that the lazy import is exercised. This is the
buildable-now half of #169; the **live vault provisioning** is Phase 2.

**A5. Silent token refresh ([#168](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/168)).**
Frontend, deploy-independent. In the axios interceptor, add the `InteractionRequiredAuthError` fallback
path after `acquireTokenSilent` (interactive `acquireTokenRedirect`/`loginRedirect`). Vitest for the
silent-fail ظْ interactive branch. Closes #168. (Real-AAD smoke is Phase 2.)

**A6. Verify the frontend container + flip the containerise task.**
Build `frontend/Dockerfile` locally, confirm nginx serves `dist` and proxies `/api/*` per
`nginx.conf`. No code change expected; flip the roadmap "Containeriseظخ" task ظش£ظْظ£à in progress.md.

### Phase 2 ظ¤ cloud apply (GATED on Owner / Azure RP registration ظ¤ do not start until cleared)
These are runbook steps, not in-repo PRs (the manifests/workflow already exist):
- Provision **ACR + ACA environment + dataq-api/worker/migrate + SWA**; set the GitHub
  secrets/vars listed in `deploy/README.md`; add the OIDC federated credential.
- **Real Key Vault** ([#169](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/169)) ظ¤ provision the
  vault, grant the api/worker managed identity *Key Vault Secrets User*, set
  `SECRET_STORE=azure_key_vault`.
- Run **Deploy** (`workflow_dispatch`) end-to-end; once green, optionally uncomment `push:[main]`
  (safe now that #382 landed).
- **Azure Monitor ظْ ADF webhook** (5 roadmap tasks): Action Group + Alert Rule for pre-prod and prod
  factories, then the **deliberate-failure smoke** (pipeline fails ظْ webhook ظْ DB ظْ UI < 5 min). Needs
  the deployed API URL; relates to surfacing the webhook URL,
  [#92](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/92).
- **Discharge the deferred live warehouse/file smoke** against the live harness (Flows A/B/C) ظ¤ flip
  the ظش£ in progress.md + note in CLAUDE.md ┬د13. Real-AAD smoke for A5 closes here too.

---

## Track B ظ¤ FastMCP server (12 tasks, epic #176). Deploy-independent ظ¤ build locally.
Per CLAUDE.md ┬د11, **do not write the MCP layer before the service layer stabilises** ظ¤ it has, so W7
is correct. Build under `backend/app/mcp/`. Suggested PR slicing:
- **B1.** Install FastMCP (pin in `requirements.txt`), scaffold `mcp_server.py`, mount at `/mcp`
  (`app.mount("/mcp", mcp.get_asgi_app())`), wire Azure AD bearer validation reusing the **same**
  `verify_azure_ad_token` as REST (no second auth path).
- **B2ظôB3.** 4 resources (`list_suites`, `get_suite_results`, `get_health_score`,
  `get_adf_pipeline_status`) + 4 tools (`trigger_suite_run`, `get_run_status`, `create_check`,
  `profile_column`) ظ¤ each a **thin wrapper over the existing service layer** (suite/run/profile/admin
  services), reusing suite-authz scoping. No business logic in the MCP layer.
- **B4.** LLM-optimised docstrings for all 8 (LLM-facing, not REST-facing ظ¤ CLAUDE.md ┬د10); E2E against
  the 4 canonical NL queries with Claude Desktop ("what failed today?", "run the orders suite on DEV",
  "why did the customer pipeline fail?", "add a null check on email").
- **B5.** Document MCP connection config (Claude Desktop / Claude.ai / Copilot / Cursor) in README;
  closes the [#17](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/17) CLAUDE.md/MCP-cadence note.

## Track C ظ¤ Consistency & transaction hardening (deploy-independent).
- **C1.** Trigger-dedup race ([#308](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/308)) ظ¤ partial
  unique index on `runs(suite_id, triggered_by)` + `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` in `_trigger_suites`.
  Backward-compatible migration (tested up/down). High-value: the live harness polls concurrently
  (webhook + poll + gap-recovery), so this race is now reachable.
- **C2.** Stuck-run reaper ([#309](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/309)) ظ¤ beat janitor
  to fail/re-dispatch runs orphaned `queued`/`running` past a threshold (gap-recovery covers
  `pipeline_runs`, not `runs`).
- **C3.** Minor robustness (no issue) ظ¤ concurrent check-edit ظْ 409 not 500; explicit `rollback` in
  `get_db` except; document the read-modify-write convention in CONTRIBUTING.

## Track D ظ¤ Hardening & docs.
- **D1.** Error-handling audit ظ¤ consistent error envelope across all endpoints (extends the existing
  `core/errors.py` envelope).
- **D2.** Endpoint metadata sweep ظ¤ every route has `summary`/`description`/`tags`/`response_model` for
  clean Swagger. Pairs with A2.
- **D3.** README + deployment guide + env-var reference; modular user docs
  ([#288](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/288)). Largely assembling `deploy/README.md`
  + `.env.app.prod.example` + per-track notes.
- **D4.** Team onboarding session + feedback (process, not code).

## Track E ظ¤ Prototype final polish (2 remaining, epic #176).
- **E1.** Dashboard KPI honesty pass ظ¤ render only real-data KPIs (pass-rate, run counts,
  `metric_value` trends); **omit** "anomalies detected" + "avg. time to resolution" (no model in v1).
- **E2.** Visual-fidelity pass ظ¤ self-hosted Inter + JetBrains Mono, motion/focus tokens, hover
  elevation, empty/loading states; keep semantic tokens so post-v1 dark mode inherits for free.

---

## Suggested order
1. **A1 ظْ A2 ظْ A6** (CI gate + prod-docs gate + verify FE image) ظ¤ fast, unblocks safe Phase-2 apply.
2. **A3, A4, A5** (App Insights, vault test, token refresh) ظ¤ finish all deploy-ready hardening.
3. **C1 ظْ C2 ظْ C3** (consistency) ظ¤ low-risk, high-value given the live polling harness.
4. **B1 ظْ B5** (FastMCP) ظ¤ the week's largest net-new surface.
5. **D1 ظْ D4, E1 ظْ E2** (docs/hardening, prototype polish) ظ¤ closeout.
6. **Phase 2 (Track A)** ظ¤ fires the moment Azure RP registration clears (Owner), in parallel with the
   above; it then unblocks the webhook tasks + the harness live-run smoke.

## Working-agreement reminders (CLAUDE.md / CONTRIBUTING)
- One functionality per commit; conventional commits; short-lived branch off `main`; **squash-merge**.
- Manually test each change before the next; from W8 tests ship with features (MCP/consistency tests now).
- Defects ظْ `gh issue create` + `Fixes #N`; revise open PRs with `fixup:` commits, never force-push.
- Backward-compatible migrations only (C1/C2): rollback plan + "tested up + down locally."
- Verify locally before push: Ruff + Black `--check` + mypy + **Bandit** + pytest + frontend lint/test.
- Run `/code-review` (the agents, not inline self-review) on every PR; file any deferred finding.
- Every issue gets label(s) + milestone; every PR gets the Week-7 milestone.

## Verification
- **Per PR:** `black --check . && ruff check . && mypy && bandit -r backend/app && pytest`
  (backend); `pnpm lint && pnpm test && pnpm build` (frontend). `pip-audit` + `pnpm audit` clean after
  A3/B1 dep adds.
- **A1:** `deploy.yml` parses; `frontend` shows `needs: backend`; `IMAGE` resolves to the SHA. (Workflow
  stays manual ظ¤ no live run.)
- **A2:** unit test ظ¤ prod ظْ `docs_url/redoc_url/openapi_url` all `None`; dev ظْ present.
- **A3:** with the connection string unset the app boots unchanged (no-op); with it mocked, spans emit.
- **A4:** mocked-SDK test exercises the `AzureKeyVaultStore` lazy-import `get`/`set` branch (coverage > 0).
- **B:** `mcp_server.py` mounts at `/mcp`; each tool returns the right shape with auth enforced; Claude
  Desktop selects the correct tool for the 4 canonical queries.
- **C1/C2:** migration up/down on Postgres; concurrent-trigger test inserts once; reaper test flips a
  stale `queued` run.
- **Phase 2 (gated):** Deploy workflow green end-to-end; deliberate ADF failure reflects in the UI < 5
  min; a harness Flow (A/B/C) runs green and persists a result row ظْ flip the deferred-smoke ظش£.

# Week 7 ظ¤ Deployment, hardening & docs ظ¤ execution plan

Two facts shape the sequencing:

1. **The external harness is live end-to-end** (HARNESS_TODO, 2026-06-27): ADF + Airflow are loading
   Snowflake + UC + flat files on real cloud accounts, mockdata cron Jobs are seeded. The *only* gate
   left on the harness is **DataQ's own cloud deploy** so its poller/check-runs can read that data ظ¤
   i.e. the deploy is the keystone that also discharges the standing "deferred live warehouse/file run
   smoke."
2. **The cloud apply is still blocked on Azure RP registration (needs Owner).** Confirmed with the user.

So the user asked to **lead with the deploy track**, but the actual `terraform apply` / ACA roll /
webhook setup cannot run until the Owner blocker clears. This plan therefore splits the deploy track

**Harness GitHub issue-filing is out of scope** (user decision); the harness stays external/not

### What #379 already shipped (don't rebuild)
- `backend/Dockerfile` **and** `frontend/Dockerfile` + `frontend/nginx.conf` ظ¤ both images exist, so

- `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` ظ¤ `workflow_dispatch`-only; `backend` (buildظْmigrateظْroll) and
  `frontend` (buildظْSWA) jobs.
- `deploy/README.md` + `deploy/.env.app.prod.example` ظ¤ topology, provisioning, GitHub config.
- `backend/app/core/secrets.py::AzureKeyVaultStore` ظ¤ coded, lazy-imported, **0% test coverage**.
- `backend/app/core/logging.py:104` ظ¤ opencensus `AzureLogHandler`, connection-string-gated.
- CORS middleware in `backend/app/main.py`, `settings.environment: Literal["dev","staging","prod"]`.

## Track A (LEAD) ظ¤ Deployment

### Phase 1 ظ¤ deploy-ready hardening (buildable now, no Azure access). One PR per item.

**A1. CI/CD safety gate ظ¤ `deploy.yml` ([#382](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/382)).**

- Add `needs: backend` to the `frontend` job (no partial deploy of new UI against an un-rolled API).

  roll the *exact same* build. Keep `workflow_dispatch`-only; do **not** uncomment `push:[main]` yet.

**A2. Prod-docs gate ([#170](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/170)).**
`backend/app/main.py:55` constructs `FastAPI(title="DataQ API", lifespan=lifespan)` with Swagger always
on. Gate it on the existing `settings.environment`:
`docs_url=None, redoc_url=None, openapi_url=None` when `environment == "prod"`, else the defaults.
Add a test asserting the three are `None` in prod and present in dev. Closes #170.

**A3. App Insights integration ظ¤ traces/errors/slow-queries/Celery metrics.**
Extend the existing connection-string-gated seam in `core/logging.py` (do **not** add a second config
path). Per ADR 0010 (vendor-neutral, App Insights = one impl behind the seam), prefer the
**`azure-monitor-opentelemetry`** distro / OTLP so the backend stays swappable ظ¤ but keep it a **no-op
when `applicationinsights_connection_string` is empty** (local/CI unaffected). Instrument: FastAPI
request spans, Celery task spans (propagating the existing `request_id`), and the SQLAlchemy slow-query
hook. Pin the new dep in `backend/requirements.txt` (single source of truth) and confirm `pip-audit`
stays clean. Tests cover the "enabled vs no-op" branch with the SDK mocked. (No GH issue ظ¤ roadmap task.)

**A4. Real-vault integration test for `AzureKeyVaultStore` ([#56 nit / #169 sibling]).**
`AzureKeyVaultStore` (secrets.py:89) is 0% covered ظ¤ the lazy-import `get`/`set` branch. Write a unit
test that mocks `azure.identity.DefaultAzureCredential` + `azure.keyvault.secrets.SecretClient` and
asserts `get`/`set` round-trip through the client and that the lazy import is exercised. This is the
buildable-now half of #169; the **live vault provisioning** is Phase 2.

**A5. Silent token refresh ([#168](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/168)).**
Frontend, deploy-independent. In the axios interceptor, add the `InteractionRequiredAuthError` fallback
path after `acquireTokenSilent` (interactive `acquireTokenRedirect`/`loginRedirect`). Vitest for the
silent-fail ظْ interactive branch. Closes #168. (Real-AAD smoke is Phase 2.)

**A6. Verify the frontend container + flip the containerise task.**
Build `frontend/Dockerfile` locally, confirm nginx serves `dist` and proxies `/api/*` per
`nginx.conf`. No code change expected; flip the roadmap "Containeriseظخ" task ظش£ظْظ£à in progress.md.

### Phase 2 ظ¤ cloud apply (GATED on Owner / Azure RP registration ظ¤ do not start until cleared)
These are runbook steps, not in-repo PRs (the manifests/workflow already exist):
- Provision **ACR + ACA environment + dataq-api/worker/migrate + SWA**; set the GitHub
  secrets/vars listed in `deploy/README.md`; add the OIDC federated credential.
- **Real Key Vault** ([#169](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/169)) ظ¤ provision the
  vault, grant the api/worker managed identity *Key Vault Secrets User*, set
  `SECRET_STORE=azure_key_vault`.
- Run **Deploy** (`workflow_dispatch`) end-to-end; once green, optionally uncomment `push:[main]`
  (safe now that #382 landed).
- **Azure Monitor ظْ ADF webhook** (5 roadmap tasks): Action Group + Alert Rule for pre-prod and prod
  factories, then the **deliberate-failure smoke** (pipeline fails ظْ webhook ظْ DB ظْ UI < 5 min). Needs
  the deployed API URL; relates to surfacing the webhook URL,
  [#92](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/92).
- **Discharge the deferred live warehouse/file smoke** against the live harness (Flows A/B/C) ظ¤ flip
  the ظش£ in progress.md + note in CLAUDE.md ┬د13. Real-AAD smoke for A5 closes here too.

---

## Track B ظ¤ FastMCP server (12 tasks, epic #176). Deploy-independent ظ¤ build locally.
Per CLAUDE.md ┬د11, **do not write the MCP layer before the service layer stabilises** ظ¤ it has, so W7
is correct. Build under `backend/app/mcp/`. Suggested PR slicing:
- **B1.** Install FastMCP (pin in `requirements.txt`), scaffold `mcp_server.py`, mount at `/mcp`
  (`app.mount("/mcp", mcp.get_asgi_app())`), wire Azure AD bearer validation reusing the **same**
  `verify_azure_ad_token` as REST (no second auth path).
- **B2ظôB3.** 4 resources (`list_suites`, `get_suite_results`, `get_health_score`,
  `get_adf_pipeline_status`) + 4 tools (`trigger_suite_run`, `get_run_status`, `create_check`,
  `profile_column`) ظ¤ each a **thin wrapper over the existing service layer** (suite/run/profile/admin
  services), reusing suite-authz scoping. No business logic in the MCP layer.
- **B4.** LLM-optimised docstrings for all 8 (LLM-facing, not REST-facing ظ¤ CLAUDE.md ┬د10); E2E against
  the 4 canonical NL queries with Claude Desktop ("what failed today?", "run the orders suite on DEV",
  "why did the customer pipeline fail?", "add a null check on email").
- **B5.** Document MCP connection config (Claude Desktop / Claude.ai / Copilot / Cursor) in README;
  closes the [#17](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/17) CLAUDE.md/MCP-cadence note.

## Track C ظ¤ Consistency & transaction hardening (deploy-independent).

  unique index on `runs(suite_id, triggered_by)` + `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` in `_trigger_suites`.
  Backward-compatible migration (tested up/down). High-value: the live harness polls concurrently
  (webhook + poll + gap-recovery), so this race is now reachable.
- **C2.** Stuck-run reaper ([#309](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/309)) ظ¤ beat janitor
  to fail/re-dispatch runs orphaned `queued`/`running` past a threshold (gap-recovery covers
  `pipeline_runs`, not `runs`).
- **C3.** Minor robustness (no issue) ظ¤ concurrent check-edit ظْ 409 not 500; explicit `rollback` in
  `get_db` except; document the read-modify-write convention in CONTRIBUTING.

- **D1.** Error-handling audit ظ¤ consistent error envelope across all endpoints (extends the existing
  `core/errors.py` envelope).
- **D2.** Endpoint metadata sweep ظ¤ every route has `summary`/`description`/`tags`/`response_model` for
  clean Swagger. Pairs with A2.

  ([#288](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/288)). Largely assembling `deploy/README.md`
  + `.env.app.prod.example` + per-track notes.

  elevation, empty/loading states; keep semantic tokens so post-v1 dark mode inherits for free.

2. **A3, A4, A5** (App Insights, vault test, token refresh) ظ¤ finish all deploy-ready hardening.
3. **C1 ظْ C2 ظْ C3** (consistency) ظ¤ low-risk, high-value given the live polling harness.
4. **B1 ظْ B5** (FastMCP) ظ¤ the week's largest net-new surface.
5. **D1 ظْ D4, E1 ظْ E2** (docs/hardening, prototype polish) ظ¤ closeout.

   above; it then unblocks the webhook tasks + the harness live-run smoke.

  Desktop selects the correct tool for the 4 canonical queries.

  min; a harness Flow (A/B/C) runs green and persists a result row ظْ flip the deferred-smoke ظش£.

content.text: So, the plan is go to cloud first then start the remsining week 7 works, is the doc conveying that?

Honest answer: **not quite.** The doc leads with the deploy *track*, but it does **not** put the actual cloud deploy first ظ¤ because you confirmed the cloud apply is still ≡ا¤ْ blocked on Azure RP registration (Owner). So the doc deliberately does the opposite of strict "cloud-first":

- It front-loads only the **buildable-now** deploy prep (Phase 1: CI gate, prod-docs gate, App Insights, vault test, token refresh).
- It sequences the **real cloud apply** (Phase 2) and everything that depends on it (webhook setup, harness live-smoke, real Key Vault) **last / in parallel** ظ¤ "fires the moment Azure RP registration clears."
- Tracks B/C/D/E (MCP, consistency, docs, polish) run **before** the cloud apply, not after.

So the current message is: *"do all the deploy-independent work now; the cloud deploy happens whenever the Owner blocker lifts."* That's the safe reading given the blocker ظ¤ true "cloud first, then the rest" would stall the entire week waiting on an external gate.

Which do you actually want?

   (prep the production deploy).
2. **Track A Phase 2** ظ¤ cloud apply (ACA/SWA) + real Key Vault [#169] + Azure Monitor webhook setup
   + deliberate-failure smoke + harness live-run (Flows A/B/C) ظْ **DataQ is LIVE in Azure** + deferred

3. **Only after deploy is live**, then in this order:
   C1 ظْ C2 ظْ C3 (consistency) ┬╖ B1 ظْ B5 (FastMCP) ┬╖ D1 ظْ D4 + E1 ظْ E2 (docs/hardening, prototype polish).

ظأبي╕ **Stall risk:** this ordering parks all of B/C/D/E behind the Owner blocker. If RP registration
slips, the only buildable work is Track A Phase 1 (hardening) ظ¤ Phase 2 and everything after wait.

# DataQ ظ¤ Week 7 working tracker (Deployment, hardening & docs)

> **NOT git-tracked** (matched by `.gitignore` ظْ `*_TODO.md`, same as `HARNESS_TODO.md`).
> Local working checklist for Week 7. Source of truth for task ticks stays
> [docs/progress.md](docs/progress.md); this is the day-to-day scratch tracker.
>
> Legend: `[ ]` todo ┬╖ `[~]` in progress ┬╖ `[x]` done ┬╖ `[-]` deferred/skipped ┬╖ ≡ا¤ْ blocked

---

## Context / sequencing

- **Harness is live end-to-end** (`HARNESS_TODO.md`, 2026-06-27): ADF + Airflow load
  Snowflake + UC + flat files; mockdata cron Jobs seeded. Only gate left on the harness =
  **DataQ's own cloud deploy** (its poller/check-runs read that data ظْ discharges the
  deferred live warehouse/file smoke).
- **STRICT CLOUD-FIRST (user decision):** Track A (Deployment) is done **in full first** ظ¤ DataQ
  must be **live in Azure before any of Tracks B/C/D/E start**. The deploy still has two internal
  phases: **Phase 1 = deploy-ready hardening (prep)** ظْ **Phase 2 = cloud apply + webhook + live
  smoke**.
- **Cloud apply is ≡ا¤ْ blocked on Azure RP registration (needs Owner)** ظ¤ this is the **gate for the
  whole week**. ظأبي╕ Strict cloud-first means everything downstream **pauses** until the Owner blocker
  clears; if RP registration slips, B/C/D/E don't proceed. (Resolving it is **Step 0** below.)
- Harness issue-filing **out of scope**.

### What #379 already shipped ظ¤ DON'T rebuild
- [x] `backend/Dockerfile` **and** `frontend/Dockerfile` + `frontend/nginx.conf`
- [x] `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` (`workflow_dispatch`-only; `backend` + `frontend` jobs)
- [x] `deploy/README.md` + `deploy/.env.app.prod.example`
- [x] `backend/app/core/secrets.py::AzureKeyVaultStore` (coded, lazy-import, **0% cov**)
- [x] `backend/app/core/logging.py:104` opencensus `AzureLogHandler` (conn-string-gated)
- [x] CORS middleware in `main.py`; `settings.environment: Literal["dev","staging","prod"]`

---

## Track A (LEAD) ظ¤ Deployment

### Phase 1 ظ¤ deploy-ready hardening (buildable now). One PR each.
- [ ] **A1 ظ¤ CI/CD safety gate (`deploy.yml`)** ┬╖ closes [#382](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/382)
  - [ ] `frontend` job gets `needs: backend` (no partial deploy vs un-rolled API)
  - [ ] pin `IMAGE` to `${{ github.sha }}` (not mutable `latest`) ظ¤ migrate/api/worker same build
  - [ ] keep `workflow_dispatch`-only (do **not** uncomment `push:[main]` yet)
- [ ] **A2 ظ¤ prod-docs gate** ┬╖ closes [#170](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/170)
  - [ ] `main.py:55` ظْ `docs_url/redoc_url/openapi_url = None` when `environment == "prod"`
  - [ ] test: prod ظْ all None; dev ظْ present
- [ ] **A3 ظ¤ App Insights integration** (no GH issue; roadmap task)
  - [ ] extend the conn-string-gated seam in `core/logging.py` (no 2nd config path)
  - [ ] prefer `azure-monitor-opentelemetry` / OTLP (ADR 0010 vendor-neutral); **no-op when
        conn string empty** (local/CI unaffected)
  - [ ] instrument FastAPI request spans + Celery task spans (propagate `request_id`) +
        slow-query hook
  - [ ] pin dep in `backend/requirements.txt`; `pip-audit` clean; mocked enabled-vs-no-op test
- [ ] **A4 ظ¤ real-vault integration test for `AzureKeyVaultStore`** (buildable half of #169; [#56 nit])
  - [ ] mock `DefaultAzureCredential` + `SecretClient`; assert `get`/`set` lazy-import branch (cov > 0)
- [ ] **A5 ظ¤ silent token refresh** ┬╖ closes [#168](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/168)
  - [ ] axios interceptor: `InteractionRequiredAuthError` ظْ interactive redirect fallback
  - [ ] vitest for silent-fail ظْ interactive branch (real-AAD smoke is Phase 2)
- [ ] **A6 ظ¤ verify frontend container + flip task**
  - [ ] build `frontend/Dockerfile`; nginx serves `dist` + proxies `/api/*`
  - [ ] flip "Containeriseظخ" ظش£ظْظ£à in `docs/progress.md`

### Phase 2 ظ¤ cloud apply ≡ا¤ْ (gated on Owner / Azure RP registration) ظ¤ runbook, not PRs
- [ ] Provision ACR + ACA env + dataq-api/worker/migrate + SWA; set GitHub secrets/vars
      (`deploy/README.md`); add OIDC federated credential
- [ ] **Real Key Vault** [#169](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/169) ظ¤ provision vault,
      grant api/worker MI *Key Vault Secrets User*, `SECRET_STORE=azure_key_vault`
- [ ] Run **Deploy** (`workflow_dispatch`) green E2E ظْ optionally uncomment `push:[main]` (safe post-A1)
- [ ] **Azure Monitor ظْ ADF webhook** (5 roadmap tasks): Action Group + Alert Rule (pre-prod + prod
      factories) ظْ deliberate-failure smoke (<5 min); relates [#92](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/92)
- [ ] **Discharge deferred live smoke** vs harness Flows A/B/C ظْ flip ظش£ in progress.md + CLAUDE.md ┬د13;
      A5 real-AAD smoke closes here

---

## Track B ظ¤ FastMCP server (12 tasks, epic #176) ظ¤ deploy-independent, `backend/app/mcp/`
- [ ] **B1** ظ¤ install FastMCP (pin in `requirements.txt`), scaffold `mcp_server.py`, mount `/mcp`
      (`app.mount("/mcp", mcp.get_asgi_app())`), reuse `verify_azure_ad_token` (no 2nd auth path)
- [ ] **B2** ظ¤ resources: `list_suites`, `get_suite_results`, `get_health_score`, `get_adf_pipeline_status`
      (thin wrappers over service layer + suite-authz scoping)
- [ ] **B3** ظ¤ tools: `trigger_suite_run`, `get_run_status`, `create_check`, `profile_column`
- [ ] **B4** ظ¤ LLM-optimised docstrings (LLM-facing, ┬د10) + Claude Desktop E2E on the 4 canonical NL
      queries
- [ ] **B5** ظ¤ README MCP connection config (Claude Desktop/Claude.ai/Copilot/Cursor); closes
      [#17](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/17)

## Track C ظ¤ Consistency & transaction hardening ظ¤ deploy-independent
- [ ] **C1** ظ¤ trigger-dedup race [#308](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/308): partial
      unique index `runs(suite_id, triggered_by)` + `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` (bwd-compat migration,
      up/down tested) ظ¤ reachable now the harness polls concurrently
- [ ] **C2** ظ¤ stuck-run reaper [#309](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/309): beat janitor
      fail/re-dispatch runs orphaned `queued`/`running` past threshold
- [ ] **C3** ظ¤ minor robustness (no issue): concurrent check-edit ظْ 409 not 500; explicit `rollback` in
      `get_db` except; document read-modify-write convention in CONTRIBUTING

## Track D ظ¤ Hardening & docs
- [ ] **D1** ظ¤ error-handling audit: consistent envelope across all endpoints (`core/errors.py`)
- [ ] **D2** ظ¤ endpoint metadata sweep: `summary`/`description`/`tags`/`response_model` everywhere (pairs A2)
- [ ] **D3** ظ¤ README + deploy guide + env-var reference; modular user docs
      [#288](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/288)
- [ ] **D4** ظ¤ team onboarding session + feedback (process, not code)

## Track E ظ¤ Prototype final polish (2 left, epic #176)
- [ ] **E1** ظ¤ Dashboard KPI honesty: real-data KPIs only (pass-rate/run counts/`metric_value` trends);
      omit "anomalies detected" + "avg. time to resolution"
- [ ] **E2** ظ¤ visual-fidelity: self-hosted Inter + JetBrains Mono, motion/focus tokens, hover elevation,
      empty/loading states; keep semantic tokens (free post-v1 dark mode)

---

## Suggested order
1. A1 ظْ A2 ظْ A6 (CI gate + prod-docs + verify FE image)
2. A3, A4, A5 (App Insights, vault test, token refresh)
3. C1 ظْ C2 ظْ C3 (consistency ظ¤ low-risk, high-value vs live polling harness)
4. B1 ظْ B5 (FastMCP ظ¤ largest net-new surface)
5. D1 ظْ D4, E1 ظْ E2 (docs/hardening, prototype polish)
6. Track A Phase 2 fires the moment Azure RP registration clears (parallel) ظْ unblocks webhook +
   harness live smoke

## Working-agreement reminders
- One functionality/commit ┬╖ conventional commits ┬╖ branch off `main` ┬╖ **squash-merge**
- Manual-test each change; tests ship with features now (MCP/consistency)
- Defects ظْ `gh issue create` + `Fixes #N`; in-review PRs use `fixup:` commits, never force-push
- Bwd-compatible migrations only (C1/C2): rollback plan + "tested up + down locally"
- Verify locally pre-push: Ruff + Black `--check` + mypy + **Bandit** + pytest + frontend lint/test;
  `pip-audit`/`pnpm audit` clean after A3/B1 dep adds
- Run `/code-review` agents on every PR; file any deferred finding
- Every issue: label(s) + milestone ┬╖ every PR: Week-7 milestone

## Verification quick-ref
- A1: `deploy.yml` parses; `frontend` has `needs: backend`; `IMAGE` = SHA (workflow stays manual)
- A2: prod ظْ docs/redoc/openapi None; dev ظْ present
- A3: conn string unset ظْ app boots unchanged; mocked ظْ spans emit
- A4: mocked-SDK test hits `AzureKeyVaultStore` lazy-import get/set (cov > 0)
- B: `/mcp` mounts; tools return right shape + auth enforced; Claude Desktop picks right tool ├ù4
- C1/C2: migration up/down on Postgres; concurrent-trigger inserts once; reaper flips stale `queued`
- Phase 2 (gated): Deploy green E2E; deliberate ADF failure ظْ UI <5 min; a harness Flow runs green ظْ
  flip deferred-smoke ظش£

content.text:
Done ظ¤ the doc now conveys strict cloud-first. Two sections changed:

1. **Context/sequencing** ظ¤ reframed: Track A (Deployment) is done **in full first**; DataQ must be **live in Azure before any of B/C/D/E start**. The Owner blocker (Azure RP registration) is now called out as the **gate for the whole week** = **Step 0**, with the explicit warning that everything downstream **pauses** until it clears.

2. **Suggested order** ظ¤ rewritten as a strict cloud-first sequence:
   - **Step 0 ≡ا¤ْ** resolve Azure RP registration (Owner)
   - **A Phase 1** deploy-ready hardening (A1ظْA6)
   - **A Phase 2** cloud apply + Key Vault + webhook + harness live-smoke ظْ **DataQ live in Azure**
   - **then** C ظْ B ظْ D/E
   - plus a ظأبي╕ **stall-risk** note: if RP registration slips, the only buildable work is Phase 1 hardening; Phase 2 and everything after wait.

One honest caveat so the plan isn't misleading: even in strict cloud-first, the **actual `terraform apply`/ACA roll cannot happen while the Owner blocker is active** ظ¤ that's a hard external gate, not a choice. So "cloud first" in practice means *Step 0 must be resolved first*, then deploy, then the rest. The doc now says exactly that rather than implying the apply can run regardless.

Want me to start on **Step 0 / A Phase 1** (the A1 CI-gate PR is the first buildable piece), or leave it here as a tracker?

content.text: I'm think about publishing the image to docker hub instead of azure container registry, what do you think? will there be any drawbacks for this?
Good question to raise *before* rewiring the deploy workflow. Let me ground it in one fact that drives the answer ظ¤ whether the image needs to be private.

input.command: gh repo view TheurgicDuke771/DataQ --json visibility,isPrivate,name 2>&1

content.content: {"isPrivate":false,"name":"DataQ","visibility":"PUBLIC"}

toolUseResult.stdout: {"isPrivate":false,"name":"DataQ","visibility":"PUBLIC"}

content.text:
Repo is **public**, which removes the biggest Docker Hub drawback (image secrecy) ظ¤ your source is already open, and per the project's no-creds-in-tracked-files rule secrets are injected at runtime (Key Vault / Container Apps secrets), not baked into the image. So a public image leaks nothing new. That said, there are real operational drawbacks for the **production pull path**:

**Drawbacks of Docker Hub vs ACR (for the prod deploy)**
1. **Pull rate limits.** Docker Hub throttles (anon 100/6h per IP, authed-free 200/6h, tightening over time). ACA autoscaling spins up replicas + the worker + the migrate job ظ¤ each a pull. Cold-start/scale-out can hit the limit and stall a rollout. ACR (and GHCR for your own images) doesn't throttle you.
2. **Auth is a stored credential, not managed identity.** ACAظ¤ACR uses a system-assigned MI with `AcrPull` ظ¤ no secret. Docker Hub means storing a username + PAT as a Container Apps registry credential ظ¤ another long-lived secret to rotate, which cuts against the "managed identity, no literals" posture in [deploy/README.md](deploy/README.md).
3. **Cross-cloud latency + outside the Azure trust boundary.** ACR co-located in your ACA region = faster pulls, no internet egress, and it sits inside Azure RBAC + Defender for Cloud vuln scanning (relevant to your pre-deploy security gate). Docker Hub pulls cross the internet and scan separately (Scout).
4. **CI rewiring.** `deploy.yml` (#379) is built around ACR (`az acr login`, `ACR_LOGIN_SERVER`/`ACR_NAME` vars). Switching means changing the login step + image refs + adding ACA registry creds for Docker Hub ظ¤ modest but real rework of apply-ready scaffolding.

**The angle in Docker Hub's favor** ظ¤ it's vendor-neutral, which actually *aligns* with your anti-lock-in ethos (ADR 0010/0013: "Azure is one impl behind each seam"), and a neutral public registry is a natural home for the **BYOL/marketplace image** (ADR 0013) you'll want post-v1.

**My recommendation: if you're moving off ACR, use GitHub Container Registry (GHCR), not Docker Hub.** It's the sweet spot for this repo:
- Free, public, **no pull-rate-limit pain** for your own images.
- Same GitHub Actions OIDC/`GITHUB_TOKEN` you already use ظ¤ **no separate account or PAT**.
- Vendor-neutral (satisfies the anti-lock-in goal Docker Hub is presumably motivating), and doubles as the BYOL distribution registry later.
- Still loses MI-based ACA auth + region co-location + Defender vs ACR ظ¤ so it's a real trade, just a better one than Docker Hub.

Net: **ACR** is best purely for in-Azure prod; **GHCR** is the best neutral alternative; **Docker Hub** is the weakest of the three here (rate limits + extra credential) and is better reserved as a *distribution* channel than the prod registry.

One process note: this changes the #379 deploy scaffolding and touches the anti-lock-in ADRs, so whichever way you go it's worth a **short ADR** (registry choice + rationale) rather than a silent swap. Want me to add a "registry decision" item to the Week-7 tracker, or draft that ADR?

content.content:
1	# ADR 0021 ظ¤ Live test/demo-data environment (retail model) lives outside the repo
2	
3	- **Status:** Accepted
4	- **Date:** 2026-06-21
5	- **Deciders:** @TheurgicDuke771
6	- **Related:** ADR [0004](0004-orchestration-abstraction.md) (orchestration abstraction ظ¤ the flows exercise it), [0010](0010-provider-agnostic-infrastructure-seams.md) / [0013](0013-marketplace-distribution-and-anti-lock-in.md) (Azure/Key Vault is one impl behind each seam; no Azure resource names in the repo), [0003](0003-gx-only-for-v1.md) (GX run paths the flows feed), [0012](0012-monitor-kind-seam.md) (the reserved freshness/volume/anomaly/schema-drift kinds the harness shapes data for), [0014](0014-reconciliation-comparison-check-kind.md) (the cross-platform `comparison` target the route map sets up). Discharges the standing **"live warehouse/file run ظ¤ deferred Week-1 smoke"** caveat carried in CLAUDE.md ┬د13 and `docs/progress.md`.
7	
8	## Context
9	
10	Every DataQ run path ظ¤ Snowflake (`SnowflakeCheckRunner`), flat-file (`FlatFileCheckRunner` + batch resolution), Unity Catalog (`UnityCatalogCheckRunner`) ظ¤ is unit-tested against canned in-memory DataFrames with the **network seam stubbed** (real GX execution, mocked IO). That is deliberate and correct for CI, but it means no run has ever touched a live warehouse, live ADLS/S3 object, or live UC table. The roadmap has carried this as the *"deferred Week-1 smoke"* since Week 1.
11	
12	Closing it needs a **realistic, repeatable data environment**: real datasources with believable data, real orchestration picking the data up, and the three DataQ run paths executing against it end-to-end. The data should model a real domain (retail) so checks, profiling, severity tiers and trends look like production, not toy fixtures.
13	
14	The question this ADR settles is **what, if anything, of that environment belongs in the DataQ source repo.**
15	
16	## Decision
17	
18	**The test/demo-data environment is a harness that lives _outside_ the DataQ source repo.** None of the setup code is committed:
19	
20	- **Terraform** that provisions Azure + Snowflake infra once accounts are enabled ظ¤ local / separate, **not git-tracked** (also keeps Azure resource names out of the repo, per ADR 0010/0013).
21	- **Mock-data generators** for the retail model and the seed data they produce ظ¤ out of repo.
22	- **The Databricks RAWظْSilverظْGold notebook** that processes landed files ظ¤ out of repo.
23	
24	What **is** git-tracked is only documentation that points at the harness and records this decision: this ADR, plus the roadmap / `progress.md` pointers that finally give the deferred-smoke caveat an owner.
25	
26	**Retail data model** the harness seeds (domains ظْ entities): Product (style, colour, material, SKU) ┬╖ Inventory (on-hand, planned) ┬╖ Location (store, address, store hours) ┬╖ Sales (placed, shipped, audited) ┬╖ Return/Cancellation (return, cancellation, guest, platform) ┬╖ Logistics (shipment, tracking, carrier) ┬╖ Guest/Customer (details, survey, chatbot feedback). Plus cross-cutting: on-sale/discount, promotion/award/coupon, tax rate, exchange rate, product price, misc.
27	
28	> **The detailed, evolving plan lives in the harness tracker, not here.** The fleshed-out data model (e.g. the Orders headerظ¤line-item split, Payments/Finance, Channel as a first-class dimension), the **key-consistency** generation discipline (the same `order_number`/`sku_id`/`tracking_number` across domains *and* across batch loads), the **dataset ظْ run-path route map** (which entity lands on Snowflake vs Unity-Catalog-plain-load vs the Databricks medallion vs flat-file), the **one-system-of-record discipline** (Snowflake `Orders` is the truth; the medallion `gold` aggregate must reconcile back to it ظ¤ the ADR-0014 `comparison` target), and the **reserved-kind data shaping** (timestamped/incremental feeds for freshness/volume/anomaly, a perturbable feed for schema-drift ظ¤ ADR 0012) are tracked in the external harness plan. This ADR records the *strategy*; that plan holds the *detail* (which would otherwise drift in a committed doc ظ¤ consistent with "only the decision is git-tracked" above).
29	
30	Data is split across **ADLS ┬╖ Snowflake ┬╖ Unity Catalog**; orchestration across **ADF ┬╖ Airflow** (both behind the existing `OrchestrationProvider` abstraction ظ¤ ADR 0004; no provider-specific branching).
31	
32	**Three reference flows** the harness drives, each exercising a distinct DataQ run path:
33	
34	- **Flow A ظ¤ load to warehouse.** File lands in ADLS ظْ ADF/Airflow picks it up ظْ loads into Snowflake / UC ظْ DataQ runs a suite against the warehouse table (`SnowflakeCheckRunner` / `UnityCatalogCheckRunner`).
35	- **Flow B ظ¤ medallion.** File lands in UC RAW ظْ Databricks notebook runs ظْ processes RAW ظْ Silver ظْ Gold ظْ DataQ runs suites against the UC tables.
36	- **Flow C ظ¤ flat-file in place.** File lands in ADLS and **stays a flat file** (no warehouse load) ظْ DataQ runs a suite directly against it (`FlatFileCheckRunner` + batch resolution picks the latest batch).
37	
38	**Hosting ظ¤ local/fast-iteration profile (chosen).** Airflow runs **locally via the official `apache/airflow` docker-compose** (webserver + scheduler + Postgres metadata DB + optional Celery workers), while **ADLS / Snowflake / Unity Catalog point at real cloud accounts**. This keeps the orchestration loop cheap and fast to iterate while the datasources stay realistic. Terraform is **not** used for the local Airflow (docker-compose is the norm; the `kreuzwerker/docker` provider is unnecessary friction); ADF, being managed, is exercised in its cloud form. A fully-cloud profile (Terraform-provisioned **Azure Managed Airflow** or AKS-hosted, alongside the rest of the infra) is a valid alternative for a more production-like run, but is **not** the v1 default. Either way Airflow stays an `OrchestrationProvider` behind the abstraction (ADR 0004) ظ¤ DataQ is agnostic to how it is hosted.
39	
40	## Consequences
41	
42	**Positive**
43	- The deferred live-warehouse/file smoke gets a concrete, owned mechanism instead of floating as a caveat.
44	- The repo stays clean of infra/seed/harness code and Azure-specific resource names (ADR 0010/0013 anti-lock-in holds).
45	- The three flows give real end-to-end coverage of all three run paths **and** the ADF + Airflow orchestration trigger-on-success path (ADR 0004), against believable retail data.
46	- Profiler, severity tiers, and Week-6 trends/anomaly baselines get realistic data to render against.
47	- The harness's parameterizable volume also backs a **performance / scale-testing** pass (post-v1): run scaling per datasource, the profiler N+1 ([#327](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/327)), and DB-growth API latency ([#323](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/323)) ظ¤ baseline-first, tracked in the harness plan, invokable by the QA/QE agent.
48	
49	**Negative / watch**
50	- The harness is unversioned alongside the product; its setup steps must be documented well enough to reproduce (README in the external harness location).
51	- **Boundary to hold:** if any harness artifact later becomes a *generic, user-facing reference template* (the way `integrations/airflow/dataq_airflow_callback.py` is a shipped template, **not** app code), it would move into `integrations/` (e.g. `integrations/databricks/`) and become git-tracked ظ¤ but the retail-specific harness stays out. This ADR governs the demo harness, not future generic templates.
52	
53	## Alternatives considered
54	
55	- **Commit the harness into the repo (e.g. `scripts/mockdata/`, `infra/terraform/`).** Rejected: bloats the product repo with environment-specific seed + Azure resource names, working against the anti-lock-in posture (ADR 0010/0013), and couples product CI to harness churn. Credentials-in-tracked-files risk too (working agreement).
56	- **Keep relying on canned-frame unit tests only.** Rejected: never discharges the live smoke; profiling/trends keep running on toy data.
57	- **Ship the Databricks notebook as an `integrations/` template now.** Deferred: it's currently the retail-specific harness, not a generalised user template. Revisit if/when a generic medallion-integration reference is wanted.
58

# ADR 0021 ظ¤ Live test/demo-data environment (retail model) lives outside the repo

- **Status:** Accepted
- **Date:** 2026-06-21
- **Deciders:** @TheurgicDuke771
- **Related:** ADR [0004](0004-orchestration-abstraction.md) (orchestration abstraction ظ¤ the flows exercise it), [0010](0010-provider-agnostic-infrastructure-seams.md) / [0013](0013-marketplace-distribution-and-anti-lock-in.md) (Azure/Key Vault is one impl behind each seam; no Azure resource names in the repo), [0003](0003-gx-only-for-v1.md) (GX run paths the flows feed), [0012](0012-monitor-kind-seam.md) (the reserved freshness/volume/anomaly/schema-drift kinds the harness shapes data for), [0014](0014-reconciliation-comparison-check-kind.md) (the cross-platform `comparison` target the route map sets up). Discharges the standing **"live warehouse/file run ظ¤ deferred Week-1 smoke"** caveat carried in CLAUDE.md ┬د13 and `docs/progress.md`.

## Context

Every DataQ run path ظ¤ Snowflake (`SnowflakeCheckRunner`), flat-file (`FlatFileCheckRunner` + batch resolution), Unity Catalog (`UnityCatalogCheckRunner`) ظ¤ is unit-tested against canned in-memory DataFrames with the **network seam stubbed** (real GX execution, mocked IO). That is deliberate and correct for CI, but it means no run has ever touched a live warehouse, live ADLS/S3 object, or live UC table. The roadmap has carried this as the *"deferred Week-1 smoke"* since Week 1.

Closing it needs a **realistic, repeatable data environment**: real datasources with believable data, real orchestration picking the data up, and the three DataQ run paths executing against it end-to-end. The data should model a real domain (retail) so checks, profiling, severity tiers and trends look like production, not toy fixtures.

The question this ADR settles is **what, if anything, of that environment belongs in the DataQ source repo.**

## Decision

**The test/demo-data environment is a harness that lives _outside_ the DataQ source repo.** None of the setup code is committed:

- **Terraform** that provisions Azure + Snowflake infra once accounts are enabled ظ¤ local / separate, **not git-tracked** (also keeps Azure resource names out of the repo, per ADR 0010/0013).
- **Mock-data generators** for the retail model and the seed data they produce ظ¤ out of repo.
- **The Databricks RAWظْSilverظْGold notebook** that processes landed files ظ¤ out of repo.

What **is** git-tracked is only documentation that points at the harness and records this decision: this ADR, plus the roadmap / `progress.md` pointers that finally give the deferred-smoke caveat an owner.

**Retail data model** the harness seeds (domains ظْ entities): Product (style, colour, material, SKU) ┬╖ Inventory (on-hand, planned) ┬╖ Location (store, address, store hours) ┬╖ Sales (placed, shipped, audited) ┬╖ Return/Cancellation (return, cancellation, guest, platform) ┬╖ Logistics (shipment, tracking, carrier) ┬╖ Guest/Customer (details, survey, chatbot feedback). Plus cross-cutting: on-sale/discount, promotion/award/coupon, tax rate, exchange rate, product price, misc.

> **The detailed, evolving plan lives in the harness tracker, not here.** The fleshed-out data model (e.g. the Orders headerظ¤line-item split, Payments/Finance, Channel as a first-class dimension), the **key-consistency** generation discipline (the same `order_number`/`sku_id`/`tracking_number` across domains *and* across batch loads), the **dataset ظْ run-path route map** (which entity lands on Snowflake vs Unity-Catalog-plain-load vs the Databricks medallion vs flat-file), the **one-system-of-record discipline** (Snowflake `Orders` is the truth; the medallion `gold` aggregate must reconcile back to it ظ¤ the ADR-0014 `comparison` target), and the **reserved-kind data shaping** (timestamped/incremental feeds for freshness/volume/anomaly, a perturbable feed for schema-drift ظ¤ ADR 0012) are tracked in the external harness plan. This ADR records the *strategy*; that plan holds the *detail* (which would otherwise drift in a committed doc ظ¤ consistent with "only the decision is git-tracked" above).

Data is split across **ADLS ┬╖ Snowflake ┬╖ Unity Catalog**; orchestration across **ADF ┬╖ Airflow** (both behind the existing `OrchestrationProvider` abstraction ظ¤ ADR 0004; no provider-specific branching).

**Three reference flows** the harness drives, each exercising a distinct DataQ run path:

- **Flow A ظ¤ load to warehouse.** File lands in ADLS ظْ ADF/Airflow picks it up ظْ loads into Snowflake / UC ظْ DataQ runs a suite against the warehouse table (`SnowflakeCheckRunner` / `UnityCatalogCheckRunner`).
- **Flow B ظ¤ medallion.** File lands in UC RAW ظْ Databricks notebook runs ظْ processes RAW ظْ Silver ظْ Gold ظْ DataQ runs suites against the UC tables.
- **Flow C ظ¤ flat-file in place.** File lands in ADLS and **stays a flat file** (no warehouse load) ظْ DataQ runs a suite directly against it (`FlatFileCheckRunner` + batch resolution picks the latest batch).

**Hosting ظ¤ local/fast-iteration profile (chosen).** Airflow runs **locally via the official `apache/airflow` docker-compose** (webserver + scheduler + Postgres metadata DB + optional Celery workers), while **ADLS / Snowflake / Unity Catalog point at real cloud accounts**. This keeps the orchestration loop cheap and fast to iterate while the datasources stay realistic. Terraform is **not** used for the local Airflow (docker-compose is the norm; the `kreuzwerker/docker` provider is unnecessary friction); ADF, being managed, is exercised in its cloud form. A fully-cloud profile (Terraform-provisioned **Azure Managed Airflow** or AKS-hosted, alongside the rest of the infra) is a valid alternative for a more production-like run, but is **not** the v1 default. Either way Airflow stays an `OrchestrationProvider` behind the abstraction (ADR 0004) ظ¤ DataQ is agnostic to how it is hosted.

## Consequences

**Positive**
- The deferred live-warehouse/file smoke gets a concrete, owned mechanism instead of floating as a caveat.
- The repo stays clean of infra/seed/harness code and Azure-specific resource names (ADR 0010/0013 anti-lock-in holds).
- The three flows give real end-to-end coverage of all three run paths **and** the ADF + Airflow orchestration trigger-on-success path (ADR 0004), against believable retail data.
- Profiler, severity tiers, and Week-6 trends/anomaly baselines get realistic data to render against.
- The harness's parameterizable volume also backs a **performance / scale-testing** pass (post-v1): run scaling per datasource, the profiler N+1 ([#327](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/327)), and DB-growth API latency ([#323](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/323)) ظ¤ baseline-first, tracked in the harness plan, invokable by the QA/QE agent.

**Negative / watch**
- The harness is unversioned alongside the product; its setup steps must be documented well enough to reproduce (README in the external harness location).
- **Boundary to hold:** if any harness artifact later becomes a *generic, user-facing reference template* (the way `integrations/airflow/dataq_airflow_callback.py` is a shipped template, **not** app code), it would move into `integrations/` (e.g. `integrations/databricks/`) and become git-tracked ظ¤ but the retail-specific harness stays out. This ADR governs the demo harness, not future generic templates.

## Alternatives considered

- **Commit the harness into the repo (e.g. `scripts/mockdata/`, `infra/terraform/`).** Rejected: bloats the product repo with environment-specific seed + Azure resource names, working against the anti-lock-in posture (ADR 0010/0013), and couples product CI to harness churn. Credentials-in-tracked-files risk too (working agreement).
- **Keep relying on canned-frame unit tests only.** Rejected: never discharges the live smoke; profiling/trends keep running on toy data.
- **Ship the Databricks notebook as an `integrations/` template now.** Deferred: it's currently the retail-specific harness, not a generalised user template. Revisit if/when a generic medallion-integration reference is wanted.

**A2. Prod-docs gate ([#170](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/170)).**
`backend/app/main.py:55` constructs `FastAPI(title="DataQ API", lifespan=lifespan)` with Swagger always
on. Gate it on the existing `settings.environment`:
`docs_url=None, redoc_url=None, openapi_url=None` when `environment == "prod"`, else the defaults.
Add a test asserting the three are `None` in prod and present in dev. Closes #170.

**A3. App Insights integration ظ¤ traces/errors/slow-queries/Celery metrics.**
Extend the existing connection-string-gated seam in `core/logging.py` (do **not** add a second config
path). Per ADR 0010 (vendor-neutral, App Insights = one impl behind the seam), prefer the
**`azure-monitor-opentelemetry`** distro / OTLP so the backend stays swappable ظ¤ but keep it a **no-op
when `applicationinsights_connection_string` is empty** (local/CI unaffected). Instrument: FastAPI
request spans, Celery task spans (propagating the existing `request_id`), and the SQLAlchemy slow-query
hook. Pin the new dep in `backend/requirements.txt` (single source of truth) and confirm `pip-audit`
stays clean. Tests cover the "enabled vs no-op" branch with the SDK mocked. (No GH issue ظ¤ roadmap task.)

**A4. Real-vault integration test for `AzureKeyVaultStore` ([#56 nit / #169 sibling]).**
`AzureKeyVaultStore` (secrets.py:89) is 0% covered ظ¤ the lazy-import `get`/`set` branch. Write a unit
test that mocks `azure.identity.DefaultAzureCredential` + `azure.keyvault.secrets.SecretClient` and
asserts `get`/`set` round-trip through the client and that the lazy import is exercised. This is the
buildable-now half of #169; the **live vault provisioning** is Phase 2.

**A5. Silent token refresh ([#168](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/168)).**

## Track A (LEAD) ظ¤ Deployment

### Phase 1 ظ¤ deploy-ready hardening (buildable now, no Azure access). One PR per item.

**A1. CI/CD safety gate ظ¤ `deploy.yml` ([#382](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/382)).**
Prerequisite for any unattended `push:[main]` deploy. In `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`:
- Add `needs: backend` to the `frontend` job (no partial deploy of new UI against an un-rolled API).
- Pin `IMAGE` to `${{ github.sha }}` instead of mutable `latest`, so the migrate job + API + worker
  roll the *exact same* build. Keep `workflow_dispatch`-only; do **not** uncomment `push:[main]` yet.
- PR closes #382. No app code; CI-only.

- Every issue gets label(s) + milestone; every PR gets the Week-7 milestone.

## Verification
- **Per PR:** `black --check . && ruff check . && mypy && bandit -r backend/app && pytest`
  (backend); `pnpm lint && pnpm test && pnpm build` (frontend). `pip-audit` + `pnpm audit` clean after
  A3/B1 dep adds.
- **A1:** `deploy.yml` parses; `frontend` shows `needs: backend`; `IMAGE` resolves to the SHA. (Workflow
  stays manual ظ¤ no live run.)
- **A2:** unit test ظ¤ prod ظْ `docs_url/redoc_url/openapi_url` all `None`; dev ظْ present.

## Appendix ظ¤ ADR 0023 draft (to be written to `docs/adr/0023-container-image-registry-ghcr.md`)

```markdown
# ADR 0023 ظ¤ Container image registry: GitHub Container Registry (GHCR) over ACR / Docker Hub

- **Status:** Accepted
- **Date:** 2026-06-27
- **Deciders:** @TheurgicDuke771
- **Related:** ADR [0010](0010-provider-agnostic-infrastructure-seams.md) (Azure is one impl behind
  each seam ظ¤ the registry is infra, not business logic), [0013](0013-marketplace-distribution-and-anti-lock-in.md)
  (BYOL/marketplace distribution ظ¤ a neutral registry doubles as the future distribution channel),
  [0021](0021-demo-test-data-environment-strategy.md) (deploy/harness context). Supersedes the ACR
  choice scaffolded in the Week-7 deploy work (#379); rides the `deploy.yml` SHA-pin (#382).

## Context

The Week-7 deploy scaffolding (#379) wired `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` to **Azure Container
Registry (ACR)**: backend image built in CI, pushed to ACR, pulled by Azure Container Apps (api +
worker share one image; a migrate Job runs `alembic upgrade head`). Before the apply, we reconsidered
the registry. The DataQ repo is **public**, and a standing project principle (ADR 0010/0013) is that
**Azure is one implementation behind each seam** ظ¤ no Azure-specific assumption should be load-bearing
where a neutral option is equivalent. A registry is pure infra; ACR couples the artifact path to Azure
for no functional gain.

Options weighed: **ACR**, **Docker Hub**, **GHCR**.

## Decision

**Use GitHub Container Registry (`ghcr.io`) as the container registry for the backend image.**

- CI pushes `ghcr.io/theurgicduke771/dataq-backend:${{ github.sha }}` (immutable SHA tag, #382),
  authenticating with the built-in `GITHUB_TOKEN` (`permissions: packages: write`) ظ¤ **no separate
  registry account or stored PAT** in CI.
- The package is published **public** (the repo is already public and the image bakes in no secrets ظ¤
  credentials are injected at runtime via Key Vault / Container Apps secrets per the working
  agreement), so **Azure Container Apps pulls anonymously ظ¤ no registry credential stored on the
  apps**. This preserves the "no literals / no long-lived registry secret" posture that ACR's managed
  identity gave us, without the Azure coupling.
- The migrate Job, api, and worker all pull the **same** SHA-pinned image.

## Consequences

**Positive**
- **Vendor-neutral** ظ¤ the artifact path no longer assumes Azure (ADR 0010/0013). If DataQ moves off
  Azure, the registry doesn't move with it.
- **Free** for public images; **no Docker Hub pull-rate throttling** on our own GHCR images (matters
  under ACA autoscaling + worker + migrate Job all pulling).
- **No extra credential** ظ¤ CI uses `GITHUB_TOKEN`; ACA pulls a public package anonymously. One fewer
  secret to rotate than Docker Hub (PAT) and parity with ACR's MI on the "no literals" goal.
- **Doubles as the BYOL/marketplace distribution registry** (ADR 0013) ظ¤ a neutral public registry is
  the natural home for a customer-pullable image post-v1.
- Same GitHub surface as the source + Actions ظ¤ one fewer system to provision/manage than ACR.

**Negative / watch**
- Loses ACR's in-region **co-location** with ACA ظْ pulls cross the internet (added cold-start/scale-out
  latency) and loses **Defender for Cloud** registry vulnerability scanning inside the Azure trust
  boundary. Mitigation: image scanning can run in CI (e.g. Trivy/Scout) independent of the registry;
  the security cadence (CLAUDE.md ┬د6) already covers SAST/deps.
- If the image ever needs to be **private** (e.g. a closed BYOL build), GHCR pull from ACA requires a
  GitHub PAT as a Container Apps registry secret ظ¤ re-introducing a stored credential. Revisit then.
- Relies on GHCR availability for deploys/scale-out (as any external registry would).

## Alternatives considered

- **Azure Container Registry (ACR).** Best purely *in-Azure*: managed-identity pull (no secret),
  region co-location, Defender scanning. Rejected as the default because it couples the artifact path
  to Azure (against ADR 0010/0013) and carries a small standing cost, for benefits that are latency/
  scanning niceties rather than correctness. Remains a valid swap if a fully-in-Azure trust boundary is
  later required ظ¤ the registry is config, not code.
- **Docker Hub.** Vendor-neutral and free for public images, but **pull-rate limits** (anon/free tiers)
  are a real risk under ACA scale-out, and it needs a **separate account + PAT** stored as an ACA
  registry credential. Strictly worse than GHCR here (GHCR has neither problem and reuses GitHub auth).
  Better reserved as an optional *additional* public distribution mirror if ever wanted.
```

input.plan:
# Week 7 ظ¤ Deployment, hardening & docs ظ¤ execution plan

## Context

Week 6 is closed (27/27); we're entering **Week 7**, whose exit gate is *"production-ready v1
deployed to Azure, CI/CD live, team onboarded."* The roadmap counts **33 Week-7 tasks** (2 done early
via #289), tracked in [docs/progress.md](../../Coding/Python/DataQ/docs/progress.md) and epic
[#176](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/176).

Two facts shape the sequencing:

1. **The external harness is live end-to-end** (HARNESS_TODO, 2026-06-27): ADF + Airflow are loading
   Snowflake + UC + flat files on real cloud accounts, mockdata cron Jobs are seeded. The *only* gate
   left on the harness is **DataQ's own cloud deploy** so its poller/check-runs can read that data ظ¤
   i.e. the deploy is the keystone that also discharges the standing "deferred live warehouse/file run
   smoke."
2. **The cloud apply is still blocked on Azure RP registration (needs Owner).** Confirmed with the user.

So the user asked to **lead with the deploy track**, but the actual `terraform apply` / ACA roll /
webhook setup cannot run until the Owner blocker clears. This plan therefore splits the deploy track
into **Phase 1 ظ¤ deploy-ready hardening (buildable + testable now, no Azure access)** and
**Phase 2 ظ¤ cloud apply (gated on Owner/RP registration)**, then sequences the remaining tracks
(FastMCP, hardening/docs, consistency, prototype polish) after.

**Harness GitHub issue-filing is out of scope** (user decision); the harness stays external/not
git-tracked.

### What #379 already shipped (don't rebuild)
- `backend/Dockerfile` **and** `frontend/Dockerfile` + `frontend/nginx.conf` ظ¤ both images exist, so
  roadmap task "Containerise FastAPI + React + Celery + Redis" is effectively **done** (progress.md
  still marks it ≡ااة ظ¤ flip to ظ£à after verifying the frontend image builds).
- `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` ظ¤ `workflow_dispatch`-only; `backend` (buildظْmigrateظْroll) and
  `frontend` (buildظْSWA) jobs.
- `deploy/README.md` + `deploy/.env.app.prod.example` ظ¤ topology, provisioning, GitHub config.
- `backend/app/core/secrets.py::AzureKeyVaultStore` ظ¤ coded, lazy-imported, **0% test coverage**.
- `backend/app/core/logging.py:104` ظ¤ opencensus `AzureLogHandler`, connection-string-gated.
- CORS middleware in `backend/app/main.py`, `settings.environment: Literal["dev","staging","prod"]`.

---

## Track A (LEAD) ظ¤ Deployment

### Phase 1 ظ¤ deploy-ready hardening (buildable now, no Azure access). One PR per item.

**A0. Container registry ظْ GHCR (decided) + ADR 0023.** Replace ACR with **GitHub Container
Registry** as the registry for the backend image (api + worker share one image). Buildable now.
- Write **`docs/adr/0023-container-image-registry-ghcr.md`** (full draft in the Appendix below);
  add it to the index in **CLAUDE.md ┬د9** + **`docs/adr/README.md`**.
- Rewire **`.github/workflows/deploy.yml`**: log in to `ghcr.io` with the built-in `GITHUB_TOKEN`
  (`packages: write` permission) instead of `az acr login`; push
  `ghcr.io/theurgicduke771/dataq-backend:${{ github.sha }}` (combine with A1's SHA pin). Drop the
  `ACR_*` vars.
- Rewire **`deploy/README.md`** + **`deploy/.env.app.prod.example`**: GHCR topology; publish the
  package **public** (repo is public, image leaks nothing) so ACA pulls **anonymously ظ¤ no stored
  registry credential** (keeps the "no literals" posture; if ever made private, ACA needs a GH PAT as a
  registry secret).
- This **overlaps A1** (both touch `deploy.yml`) ظ¤ land them as one PR or A0 first, A1 rebased.

**A1. CI/CD safety gate ظ¤ `deploy.yml` ([#382](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/382)).**
Prerequisite for any unattended `push:[main]` deploy. In `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`:
- Add `needs: backend` to the `frontend` job (no partial deploy of new UI against an un-rolled API).
- Pin `IMAGE` to `${{ github.sha }}` instead of mutable `latest`, so the migrate job + API + worker
  roll the *exact same* build. Keep `workflow_dispatch`-only; do **not** uncomment `push:[main]` yet.
- PR closes #382. No app code; CI-only.

**A2. Prod-docs gate ([#170](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/170)).**
`backend/app/main.py:55` constructs `FastAPI(title="DataQ API", lifespan=lifespan)` with Swagger always
on. Gate it on the existing `settings.environment`:
`docs_url=None, redoc_url=None, openapi_url=None` when `environment == "prod"`, else the defaults.
Add a test asserting the three are `None` in prod and present in dev. Closes #170.

**A3. App Insights integration ظ¤ traces/errors/slow-queries/Celery metrics.**
Extend the existing connection-string-gated seam in `core/logging.py` (do **not** add a second config
path). Per ADR 0010 (vendor-neutral, App Insights = one impl behind the seam), prefer the
**`azure-monitor-opentelemetry`** distro / OTLP so the backend stays swappable ظ¤ but keep it a **no-op
when `applicationinsights_connection_string` is empty** (local/CI unaffected). Instrument: FastAPI
request spans, Celery task spans (propagating the existing `request_id`), and the SQLAlchemy slow-query
hook. Pin the new dep in `backend/requirements.txt` (single source of truth) and confirm `pip-audit`
stays clean. Tests cover the "enabled vs no-op" branch with the SDK mocked. (No GH issue ظ¤ roadmap task.)

**A4. Real-vault integration test for `AzureKeyVaultStore` ([#56 nit / #169 sibling]).**
`AzureKeyVaultStore` (secrets.py:89) is 0% covered ظ¤ the lazy-import `get`/`set` branch. Write a unit
test that mocks `azure.identity.DefaultAzureCredential` + `azure.keyvault.secrets.SecretClient` and
asserts `get`/`set` round-trip through the client and that the lazy import is exercised. This is the
buildable-now half of #169; the **live vault provisioning** is Phase 2.

**A5. Silent token refresh ([#168](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/168)).**
Frontend, deploy-independent. In the axios interceptor, add the `InteractionRequiredAuthError` fallback
path after `acquireTokenSilent` (interactive `acquireTokenRedirect`/`loginRedirect`). Vitest for the
silent-fail ظْ interactive branch. Closes #168. (Real-AAD smoke is Phase 2.)

**A6. Verify the frontend container + flip the containerise task.**
Build `frontend/Dockerfile` locally, confirm nginx serves `dist` and proxies `/api/*` per
`nginx.conf`. No code change expected; flip the roadmap "Containeriseظخ" task ظش£ظْظ£à in progress.md.

### Phase 2 ظ¤ cloud apply (GATED on Owner / Azure RP registration ظ¤ do not start until cleared)
These are runbook steps, not in-repo PRs (the manifests/workflow already exist):
- Provision **ACR + ACA environment + dataq-api/worker/migrate + SWA**; set the GitHub
  secrets/vars listed in `deploy/README.md`; add the OIDC federated credential.

## Appendix ظ¤ ADR 0023 draft (to be written to `docs/adr/0023-container-image-registry-ghcr.md`)

```markdown
# ADR 0023 ظ¤ Container image registry: GitHub Container Registry (GHCR) over ACR / Docker Hub

- **Status:** Accepted
- **Date:** 2026-06-27
- **Deciders:** @TheurgicDuke771
- **Related:** ADR [0010](0010-provider-agnostic-infrastructure-seams.md) (Azure is one impl behind
  each seam ظ¤ the registry is infra, not business logic), [0013](0013-marketplace-distribution-and-anti-lock-in.md)
  (BYOL/marketplace distribution ظ¤ a neutral registry doubles as the future distribution channel),
  [0021](0021-demo-test-data-environment-strategy.md) (deploy/harness context). Supersedes the ACR
  choice scaffolded in the Week-7 deploy work (#379); rides the `deploy.yml` SHA-pin (#382).

## Context

The Week-7 deploy scaffolding (#379) wired `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` to **Azure Container
Registry (ACR)**: backend image built in CI, pushed to ACR, pulled by Azure Container Apps (api +
worker share one image; a migrate Job runs `alembic upgrade head`). Before the apply, we reconsidered
the registry. The DataQ repo is **public**, and a standing project principle (ADR 0010/0013) is that
**Azure is one implementation behind each seam** ظ¤ no Azure-specific assumption should be load-bearing
where a neutral option is equivalent. A registry is pure infra; ACR couples the artifact path to Azure
for no functional gain.

Options weighed: **ACR**, **Docker Hub**, **GHCR**.

## Decision

**Use GitHub Container Registry (`ghcr.io`) as the container registry for the backend image.**

- CI pushes `ghcr.io/theurgicduke771/dataq-backend:${{ github.sha }}` (immutable SHA tag, #382),
  authenticating with the built-in `GITHUB_TOKEN` (`permissions: packages: write`) ظ¤ **no separate
  registry account or stored PAT** in CI.
- The package is published **public** (the repo is already public and the image bakes in no secrets ظ¤
  credentials are injected at runtime via Key Vault / Container Apps secrets per the working
  agreement), so **Azure Container Apps pulls anonymously ظ¤ no registry credential stored on the
  apps**. This preserves the "no literals / no long-lived registry secret" posture that ACR's managed
  identity gave us, without the Azure coupling.
- The migrate Job, api, and worker all pull the **same** SHA-pinned image.

## Consequences

**Positive**
- **Vendor-neutral** ظ¤ the artifact path no longer assumes Azure (ADR 0010/0013). If DataQ moves off
  Azure, the registry doesn't move with it.
- **Free** for public images; **no Docker Hub pull-rate throttling** on our own GHCR images (matters
  under ACA autoscaling + worker + migrate Job all pulling).
- **No extra credential** ظ¤ CI uses `GITHUB_TOKEN`; ACA pulls a public package anonymously. One fewer
  secret to rotate than Docker Hub (PAT) and parity with ACR's MI on the "no literals" goal.
- **Doubles as the BYOL/marketplace distribution registry** (ADR 0013) ظ¤ a neutral public registry is
  the natural home for a customer-pullable image post-v1.
- Same GitHub surface as the source + Actions ظ¤ one fewer system to provision/manage than ACR.

**Negative / watch**
- Loses ACR's in-region **co-location** with ACA ظْ pulls cross the internet (added cold-start/scale-out
  latency) and loses **Defender for Cloud** registry vulnerability scanning inside the Azure trust
  boundary. Mitigation: image scanning can run in CI (e.g. Trivy/Scout) independent of the registry;
  the security cadence (CLAUDE.md ┬د6) already covers SAST/deps.
- If the image ever needs to be **private** (e.g. a closed BYOL build), GHCR pull from ACA requires a
  GitHub PAT as a Container Apps registry secret ظ¤ re-introducing a stored credential. Revisit then.
- Relies on GHCR availability for deploys/scale-out (as any external registry would).

## Alternatives considered

- **Azure Container Registry (ACR).** Best purely *in-Azure*: managed-identity pull (no secret),
  region co-location, Defender scanning. Rejected as the default because it couples the artifact path
  to Azure (against ADR 0010/0013) and carries a small standing cost, for benefits that are latency/
  scanning niceties rather than correctness. Remains a valid swap if a fully-in-Azure trust boundary is
  later required ظ¤ the registry is config, not code.
- **Docker Hub.** Vendor-neutral and free for public images, but **pull-rate limits** (anon/free tiers)
  are a real risk under ACA scale-out, and it needs a **separate account + PAT** stored as an ACA
  registry credential. Strictly worse than GHCR here (GHCR has neither problem and reuses GitHub auth).
  Better reserved as an optional *additional* public distribution mirror if ever wanted.
```

## Appendix ظ¤ ADR 0023 draft (to be written to `docs/adr/0023-container-image-registry-ghcr.md`)

```markdown
# ADR 0023 ظ¤ Container image registry: GitHub Container Registry (GHCR) over ACR / Docker Hub

- **Status:** Accepted
- **Date:** 2026-06-27
- **Deciders:** @TheurgicDuke771
- **Related:** ADR [0010](0010-provider-agnostic-infrastructure-seams.md) (Azure is one impl behind
  each seam ظ¤ the registry is infra, not business logic), [0013](0013-marketplace-distribution-and-anti-lock-in.md)
  (BYOL/marketplace distribution ظ¤ a neutral registry doubles as the future distribution channel),
  [0021](0021-demo-test-data-environment-strategy.md) (deploy/harness context). Supersedes the ACR
  choice scaffolded in the Week-7 deploy work (#379); rides the `deploy.yml` SHA-pin (#382).

## Context

The Week-7 deploy scaffolding (#379) wired `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` to **Azure Container
Registry (ACR)**: backend image built in CI, pushed to ACR, pulled by Azure Container Apps (api +
worker share one image; a migrate Job runs `alembic upgrade head`). Before the apply, we reconsidered
the registry. The DataQ repo is **public**, and a standing project principle (ADR 0010/0013) is that
**Azure is one implementation behind each seam** ظ¤ no Azure-specific assumption should be load-bearing
where a neutral option is equivalent. A registry is pure infra; ACR couples the artifact path to Azure
for no functional gain.

Options weighed: **ACR**, **Docker Hub**, **GHCR**.

## Decision

**Use GitHub Container Registry (`ghcr.io`) as the container registry for the backend image.**

- CI pushes `ghcr.io/theurgicduke771/dataq-backend:${{ github.sha }}` (immutable SHA tag, #382),
  authenticating with the built-in `GITHUB_TOKEN` (`permissions: packages: write`) ظ¤ **no separate
  registry account or stored PAT** in CI.
- The package is published **public** (the repo is already public and the image bakes in no secrets ظ¤
  credentials are injected at runtime via Key Vault / Container Apps secrets per the working
  agreement), so **Azure Container Apps pulls anonymously ظ¤ no registry credential stored on the
  apps**. This preserves the "no literals / no long-lived registry secret" posture that ACR's managed
  identity gave us, without the Azure coupling.
- The migrate Job, api, and worker all pull the **same** SHA-pinned image.

## Consequences

**Positive**
- **Vendor-neutral** ظ¤ the artifact path no longer assumes Azure (ADR 0010/0013). If DataQ moves off
  Azure, the registry doesn't move with it.
- **Free** for public images; **no Docker Hub pull-rate throttling** on our own GHCR images (matters
  under ACA autoscaling + worker + migrate Job all pulling).
- **No extra credential** ظ¤ CI uses `GITHUB_TOKEN`; ACA pulls a public package anonymously. One fewer
  secret to rotate than Docker Hub (PAT) and parity with ACR's MI on the "no literals" goal.
- **Doubles as the BYOL/marketplace distribution registry** (ADR 0013) ظ¤ a neutral public registry is
  the natural home for a customer-pullable image post-v1.
- Same GitHub surface as the source + Actions ظ¤ one fewer system to provision/manage than ACR.

**Negative / watch**
- Loses ACR's in-region **co-location** with ACA ظْ pulls cross the internet (added cold-start/scale-out
  latency) and loses **Defender for Cloud** registry vulnerability scanning inside the Azure trust
  boundary. Mitigation: image scanning can run in CI (e.g. Trivy/Scout) independent of the registry;
  the security cadence (CLAUDE.md ┬د6) already covers SAST/deps.
- If the image ever needs to be **private** (e.g. a closed BYOL build), GHCR pull from ACA requires a
  GitHub PAT as a Container Apps registry secret ظ¤ re-introducing a stored credential. Revisit then.
- Relies on GHCR availability for deploys/scale-out (as any external registry would).

## Alternatives considered

- **Azure Container Registry (ACR).** Best purely *in-Azure*: managed-identity pull (no secret),
  region co-location, Defender scanning. Rejected as the default because it couples the artifact path
  to Azure (against ADR 0010/0013) and carries a small standing cost, for benefits that are latency/
  scanning niceties rather than correctness. Remains a valid swap if a fully-in-Azure trust boundary is
  later required ظ¤ the registry is config, not code.
- **Docker Hub.** Vendor-neutral and free for public images, but **pull-rate limits** (anon/free tiers)
  are a real risk under ACA scale-out, and it needs a **separate account + PAT** stored as an ACA
  registry credential. Strictly worse than GHCR here (GHCR has neither problem and reuses GitHub auth).
  Better reserved as an optional *additional* public distribution mirror if ever wanted.
```
